authors_text: Fatih Kamisli
title: End-to-End Learned Block-Based Image Compression with Block-Level Masked Convolutions and Asymptotic Closed Loop Training
title_zh: 具有块级掩蔽卷积和渐近闭环训练的端到端学习的基于块的图像压缩
abstract: Learned image compression research has achieved state-of-the-art compression performance with auto-encoder based neural network architectures, where the image is mapped via convolutional neural networks (CNN) into a latent representation that is quantized and processed again with CNN to obtain the reconstructed image. CNN operate on entire input images. On the other hand, traditional state-of-the-art image and video compression methods process images with a block-by-block processing approach for various reasons. Very recently, work on learned image compression with block based approaches have also appeared, which use the auto-encoder architecture on large blocks of the input image and introduce additional neural networks that perform intra/spatial prediction and deblocking/post-processing functions. This paper explores an alternative learned block-based image compression approach in which neither an explicit intra prediction neural network nor an explicit deblocking neural network is used. A single auto-encoder neural network with block-level masked convolutions is used and the block size is much smaller (8x8). By using block-level masked convolutions, each block is processed using reconstructed neighboring left and upper blocks both at the encoder and decoder. Hence, the mutual information between adjacent blocks is exploited during compression and each block is reconstructed using neighboring blocks, resolving the need for explicit intra prediction and deblocking neural networks. Since the explored system is a closed loop system, a special optimization procedure, the asymptotic closed loop design, is used with standard stochastic gradient descent based training. The experimental results indicate competitive image compression performance.
summary: Learned image compression research has achieved state-of-the-art compression performance with auto-encoder based neural network architectures, where the image is mapped via convolutional neural networks (CNN) into a latent representation that is quantized and processed again with CNN to obtain the reconstructed image. CNN operate on entire input images. On the other hand, traditional state-of-the-art image and video compression methods process images with a block-by-block processing approach for various reasons. Very recently, work on learned image compression with block based approaches have also appeared, which use the auto-encoder architecture on large blocks of the input image and introduce additional neural networks that perform intra/spatial prediction and deblocking/post-processing functions. This paper explores an alternative learned block-based image compression approach in which neither an explicit intra prediction neural network nor an explicit deblocking neural network is used. A single auto-encoder neural network with block-level masked convolutions is used and the block size is much smaller (8x8). By using block-level masked convolutions, each block is processed using reconstructed neighboring left and upper blocks both at the encoder and decoder. Hence, the mutual information between adjacent blocks is exploited during compression and each block is reconstructed using neighboring blocks, resolving the need for explicit intra prediction and deblocking neural networks. Since the explored system is a closed loop system, a special optimization procedure, the asymptotic closed loop design, is used with standard stochastic gradient descent based training. The experimental results indicate competitive image compression performance.
abstract_zh: 学习图像压缩研究已经通过基于自动编码器的神经网络架构实现了最先进的压缩性能，其中图像通过卷积神经网络（CNN）映射到潜在表示，然后用 CNN 再次量化和处理以获得重建图像。 CNN 对整个输入图像进行操作。另一方面，由于各种原因，传统的最先进的图像和视频压缩方法采用逐块处理方法来处理图像。最近，还出现了基于块的方法进行学习图像压缩的工作，该方法在输入图像的大块上使用自动编码器架构，并引入执行帧内/空间预测和解块/后处理功能的附加神经网络。本文探索了另一种基于学习块的图像压缩方法，其中既不使用显式帧内预测神经网络，也不使用显式解块神经网络。使用具有块级掩码卷积的单个自动编码器神经网络，并且块大小要小得多（8x8）。通过使用块级掩码卷积，每个块都在编码器和解码器处使用重建的相邻左块和上块进行处理。因此，在压缩期间利用相邻块之间的互信息，并且使用相邻块重建每个块，从而解决了对显式帧内预测和解块神经网络的需求。由于所探索的系统是闭环系统，因此特殊的优化程序（渐近闭环设计）与基于标准随机梯度下降的训练一起使用。实验结果显示了具有竞争力的图像压缩性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于块级掩码卷积和渐近闭环训练的端到端学习图像压缩方法，无需显式预测网络即可实现高效压缩。
AI.motivation: 传统分块压缩依赖显式预测网络，而现有学习方法多采用全图像处理，难以有效利用块间信息。本文旨在通过块级掩码卷积直接建模相邻块关联，简化网络结构。
AI.method: 使用8x8小块划分图像，设计块级掩码卷积使编码器/解码器可访问左侧和上方已重建块信息，结合渐近闭环训练优化端到端压缩流程。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在压缩性能上达到现有技术水平，验证了块间信息建模的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过块级掩码卷积和闭环训练，可无需显式预测网络实现高效图像压缩，为压缩算法设计提供新思路。

search_text: End-to-End Learned Block-Based Image Compression with Block-Level Masked Convolutions and Asymptotic Closed Loop Training 具有块级掩蔽卷积和渐近闭环训练的端到端学习的基于块的图像压缩 Learned image compression research has achieved state-of-the-art compression performance with auto-encoder based neural network architectures, where the image is mapped via convolutional neural networks (CNN) into a latent representation that is quantized and processed again with CNN to obtain the reconstructed image. CNN operate on entire input images. On the other hand, traditional state-of-the-art image and video compression methods process images with a block-by-block processing approach for various reasons. Very recently, work on learned image compression with block based approaches have also appeared, which use the auto-encoder architecture on large blocks of the input image and introduce additional neural networks that perform intra/spatial prediction and deblocking/post-processing functions. This paper explores an alternative learned block-based image compression approach in which neither an explicit intra prediction neural network nor an explicit deblocking neural network is used. A single auto-encoder neural network with block-level masked convolutions is used and the block size is much smaller (8x8). By using block-level masked convolutions, each block is processed using reconstructed neighboring left and upper blocks both at the encoder and decoder. Hence, the mutual information between adjacent blocks is exploited during compression and each block is reconstructed using neighboring blocks, resolving the need for explicit intra prediction and deblocking neural networks. Since the explored system is a closed loop system, a special optimization procedure, the asymptotic closed loop design, is used with standard stochastic gradient descent based training. The experimental results indicate competitive image compression performance. 学习图像压缩研究已经通过基于自动编码器的神经网络架构实现了最先进的压缩性能，其中图像通过卷积神经网络（CNN）映射到潜在表示，然后用 CNN 再次量化和处理以获得重建图像。 CNN 对整个输入图像进行操作。另一方面，由于各种原因，传统的最先进的图像和视频压缩方法采用逐块处理方法来处理图像。最近，还出现了基于块的方法进行学习图像压缩的工作，该方法在输入图像的大块上使用自动编码器架构，并引入执行帧内/空间预测和解块/后处理功能的附加神经网络。本文探索了另一种基于学习块的图像压缩方法，其中既不使用显式帧内预测神经网络，也不使用显式解块神经网络。使用具有块级掩码卷积的单个自动编码器神经网络，并且块大小要小得多（8x8）。通过使用块级掩码卷积，每个块都在编码器和解码器处使用重建的相邻左块和上块进行处理。因此，在压缩期间利用相邻块之间的互信息，并且使用相邻块重建每个块，从而解决了对显式帧内预测和解块神经网络的需求。由于所探索的系统是闭环系统，因此特殊的优化程序（渐近闭环设计）与基于标准随机梯度下降的训练一起使用。实验结果显示了具有竞争力的图像压缩性能。 本文提出一种基于块级掩码卷积和渐近闭环训练的端到端学习图像压缩方法，无需显式预测网络即可实现高效压缩。 Fatih Kamisli eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.MM

authors_text: Mengqi Xue,Haofei Zhang,Jie Song,Mingli Song
title: Meta-attention for ViT-backed Continual Learning
title_zh: ViT 支持的持续学习的元注意力
abstract: Continual learning is a longstanding research topic due to its crucial role in tackling continually arriving tasks. Up to now, the study of continual learning in computer vision is mainly restricted to convolutional neural networks (CNNs). However, recently there is a tendency that the newly emerging vision transformers (ViTs) are gradually dominating the field of computer vision, which leaves CNN-based continual learning lagging behind as they can suffer from severe performance degradation if straightforwardly applied to ViTs. In this paper, we study ViT-backed continual learning to strive for higher performance riding on recent advances of ViTs. Inspired by mask-based continual learning methods in CNNs, where a mask is learned per task to adapt the pre-trained ViT to the new task, we propose MEta-ATtention (MEAT), i.e., attention to self-attention, to adapt a pre-trained ViT to new tasks without sacrificing performance on already learned tasks. Unlike prior mask-based methods like Piggyback, where all parameters are associated with corresponding masks, MEAT leverages the characteristics of ViTs and only masks a portion of its parameters. It renders MEAT more efficient and effective with less overhead and higher accuracy. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MEAT exhibits significant superiority to its state-of-the-art CNN counterparts, with 4.0~6.0% absolute boosts in accuracy. Our code has been released at https://github.com/zju-vipa/MEAT-TIL.
summary: Continual learning is a longstanding research topic due to its crucial role in tackling continually arriving tasks. Up to now, the study of continual learning in computer vision is mainly restricted to convolutional neural networks (CNNs). However, recently there is a tendency that the newly emerging vision transformers (ViTs) are gradually dominating the field of computer vision, which leaves CNN-based continual learning lagging behind as they can suffer from severe performance degradation if straightforwardly applied to ViTs. In this paper, we study ViT-backed continual learning to strive for higher performance riding on recent advances of ViTs. Inspired by mask-based continual learning methods in CNNs, where a mask is learned per task to adapt the pre-trained ViT to the new task, we propose MEta-ATtention (MEAT), i.e., attention to self-attention, to adapt a pre-trained ViT to new tasks without sacrificing performance on already learned tasks. Unlike prior mask-based methods like Piggyback, where all parameters are associated with corresponding masks, MEAT leverages the characteristics of ViTs and only masks a portion of its parameters. It renders MEAT more efficient and effective with less overhead and higher accuracy. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MEAT exhibits significant superiority to its state-of-the-art CNN counterparts, with 4.0~6.0% absolute boosts in accuracy. Our code has been released at https://github.com/zju-vipa/MEAT-TIL.
abstract_zh: 持续学习是一个长期存在的研究课题，因为它在处理不断到达的任务中发挥着至关重要的作用。到目前为止，计算机视觉中持续学习的研究主要局限于卷积神经网络（CNN）。然而，最近出现的趋势是，新兴的视觉变换器（ViT）逐渐在计算机视觉领域占据主导地位，这使得基于 CNN 的持续学习落后了，因为如果直接应用于 ViT，它们可能会遭受严重的性能下降。在本文中，我们研究了 ViT 支持的持续学习，以利用 ViT 的最新进展来争取更高的性能。受 CNN 中基于掩码的持续学习方法的启发，每个任务都会学习一个掩码，以使预训练的 ViT 适应新任务，我们提出了 MEta-ATtention (MEAT)，即对自注意力的关注，使预训练的 ViT 适应新任务，而不牺牲已学习任务的性能。与之前基于掩码的方法（如 Piggyback）不同，MEAT 利用了 ViT 的特性，仅掩蔽其部分参数。 It renders MEAT more efficient and effective with less overhead and higher accuracy.大量实验表明，MEAT 比最先进的 CNN 模型表现出显着的优越性，准确率绝对提高了 4.0~6.0%。我们的代码已发布在https://github.com/zju-vipa/MEAT-TIL。

AI.tldr: 本文研究基于视觉Transformer（ViT）的持续学习方法，提出了一种名为MEAT的元注意力机制，以在不牺牲已有任务性能的情况下适应新任务。
AI.motivation: 尽管卷积神经网络（CNN）在持续学习领域已有较多研究，但新兴的视觉Transformer（ViT）在计算机视觉中逐渐占据主导地位，而直接将CNN的持续学习方法应用于ViT会导致性能显著下降，因此需要针对ViT设计新的持续学习方案。
AI.method: 受CNN中基于掩码的持续学习方法启发，本文提出MEAT方法，通过仅对ViT的部分参数进行掩码操作，利用ViT的结构特性实现高效且准确的适应新任务，避免了传统掩码方法对所有参数进行关联的高开销问题。
AI.result: 实验表明，MEAT在准确性上相比最先进的CNN持续学习方法提升了4.0~6.0%，且代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: MEAT通过元注意力机制有效解决了ViT在持续学习中的性能退化问题，验证了其在效率和效果上的优越性，为ViT与持续学习的结合提供了新思路。

authors_text: Ali Borji
title: CNNs and Transformers Perceive Hybrid Images Similar to Humans
title_zh: CNN 和 Transformers 感知与人类相似的混合图像
abstract: Hybrid images is a technique to generate images with two interpretations that change as a function of viewing distance. It has been utilized to study multiscale processing of images by the human visual system. Using 63,000 hybrid images across 10 fruit categories, here we show that predictions of deep learning vision models qualitatively matches with the human perception of these images. Our results provide yet another evidence in support of the hypothesis that Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers are good at modeling the feedforward sweep of information in the ventral stream of visual cortex. Code and data is available at https://github.com/aliborji/hybrid_images.git.
summary: Hybrid images is a technique to generate images with two interpretations that change as a function of viewing distance. It has been utilized to study multiscale processing of images by the human visual system. Using 63,000 hybrid images across 10 fruit categories, here we show that predictions of deep learning vision models qualitatively matches with the human perception of these images. Our results provide yet another evidence in support of the hypothesis that Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers are good at modeling the feedforward sweep of information in the ventral stream of visual cortex. Code and data is available at https://github.com/aliborji/hybrid_images.git.
abstract_zh: 混合图像是一种生成具有两种解释的图像的技术，两种解释随着观看距离的函数而变化。它已被用来研究人类视觉系统对图像的多尺度处理。在这里，我们使用 10 个水果类别的 63,000 张混合图像，展示了深度学习视觉模型的预测在质量上与人类对这些图像的感知相匹配。我们的结果提供了另一个证据来支持以下假设：卷积神经网络（CNN）和 Transformer 擅长对视觉皮层腹侧流中的信息前馈扫描进行建模。代码和数据可在 https://github.com/aliborji/hybrid_images.git 获取。

search_text: CNNs and Transformers Perceive Hybrid Images Similar to Humans CNN 和 Transformers 感知与人类相似的混合图像 Hybrid images is a technique to generate images with two interpretations that change as a function of viewing distance. It has been utilized to study multiscale processing of images by the human visual system. Using 63,000 hybrid images across 10 fruit categories, here we show that predictions of deep learning vision models qualitatively matches with the human perception of these images. Our results provide yet another evidence in support of the hypothesis that Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers are good at modeling the feedforward sweep of information in the ventral stream of visual cortex. Code and data is available at https://github.com/aliborji/hybrid_images.git. 混合图像是一种生成具有两种解释的图像的技术，两种解释随着观看距离的函数而变化。它已被用来研究人类视觉系统对图像的多尺度处理。在这里，我们使用 10 个水果类别的 63,000 张混合图像，展示了深度学习视觉模型的预测在质量上与人类对这些图像的感知相匹配。我们的结果提供了另一个证据来支持以下假设：卷积神经网络（CNN）和 Transformer 擅长对视觉皮层腹侧流中的信息前馈扫描进行建模。代码和数据可在 https://github.com/aliborji/hybrid_images.git 获取。 研究CNN和Transformer模型在处理混合图像时是否与人类感知一致 Ali Borji cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Zhe Zhang,Yaozhong Gan,Xiaoyang Tan
title: Robust Action Gap Increasing with Clipped Advantage Learning
title_zh: 强大的行动差距随着优势学习的削减而增加
abstract: Advantage Learning (AL) seeks to increase the action gap between the optimal action and its competitors, so as to improve the robustness to estimation errors. However, the method becomes problematic when the optimal action induced by the approximated value function does not agree with the true optimal action. In this paper, we present a novel method, named clipped Advantage Learning (clipped AL), to address this issue. The method is inspired by our observation that increasing the action gap blindly for all given samples while not taking their necessities into account could accumulate more errors in the performance loss bound, leading to a slow value convergence, and to avoid that, we should adjust the advantage value adaptively. We show that our simple clipped AL operator not only enjoys fast convergence guarantee but also retains proper action gaps, hence achieving a good balance between the large action gap and the fast convergence. The feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method are verified empirically on several RL benchmarks with promising performance.
summary: Advantage Learning (AL) seeks to increase the action gap between the optimal action and its competitors, so as to improve the robustness to estimation errors. However, the method becomes problematic when the optimal action induced by the approximated value function does not agree with the true optimal action. In this paper, we present a novel method, named clipped Advantage Learning (clipped AL), to address this issue. The method is inspired by our observation that increasing the action gap blindly for all given samples while not taking their necessities into account could accumulate more errors in the performance loss bound, leading to a slow value convergence, and to avoid that, we should adjust the advantage value adaptively. We show that our simple clipped AL operator not only enjoys fast convergence guarantee but also retains proper action gaps, hence achieving a good balance between the large action gap and the fast convergence. The feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method are verified empirically on several RL benchmarks with promising performance.
abstract_zh: 优势学习（AL）旨在增加最优动作与其竞争对手之间的动作差距，从而提高对估计误差的鲁棒性。然而，当近似值函数得出的最优动作与真实的最优动作不一致时，该方法就会出现问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的方法，称为剪辑优势学习（clipped AL）来解决这个问题。该方法的灵感来自于我们的观察，即盲目地增加所有给定样本的动作间隙而不考虑其必要性可能会在性能损失范围内积累更多错误，导致值收敛缓慢，为了避免这种情况，我们应该自适应地调整优势值。我们表明，我们的简单裁剪 AL 算子不仅享有快速收敛保证，而且保留了适当的动作间隙，从而在大动作间隙和快速收敛之间实现了良好的平衡。该方法的可行性和有效性在几个具有良好性能的 RL 基准上得到了实证验证。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为剪辑优势学习（clipped AL）的新方法，旨在通过自适应调整优势值来解决优势学习（AL）在估计误差下的鲁棒性问题，并在强化学习基准测试中验证了其有效性。
AI.motivation: 传统优势学习（AL）在增加最优动作与竞争者之间的动作差距时，可能因近似值函数诱导的最优动作与真实最优动作不一致而导致误差累积和收敛速度变慢，需改进方法以平衡动作差距与收敛效率。
AI.method: 通过引入剪辑操作符，自适应调整优势值，避免盲目增加所有样本的动作差距，从而在保持合理动作差距的同时加速值函数收敛。
AI.result: 在多个强化学习基准测试中，剪辑AL方法表现出良好的性能，验证了其可行性与有效性。
AI.conclusion: 剪辑AL方法通过平衡动作差距与收敛速度，为强化学习提供了更鲁棒的解决方案。

However, the method becomes problematic when the optimal action induced by the approximated value function does not agree with the true optimal action. In this paper, we present a novel method, named clipped Advantage Learning (clipped AL), to address this issue. The method is inspired by our observation that increasing the action gap blindly for all given samples while not taking their necessities into account could accumulate more errors in the performance loss bound, leading to a slow value convergence, and to avoid that, we should adjust the advantage value adaptively. We show that our simple clipped AL operator not only enjoys fast convergence guarantee but also retains proper action gaps, hence achieving a good balance between the large action gap and the fast convergence. The feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method are verified empirically on several RL benchmarks with promising performance. 优势学习（AL）旨在增加最优动作与其竞争对手之间的动作差距，从而提高对估计误差的鲁棒性。然而，当近似值函数得出的最优动作与真实的最优动作不一致时，该方法就会出现问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的方法，称为剪辑优势学习（clipped AL）来解决这个问题。该方法的灵感来自于我们的观察，即盲目地增加所有给定样本的动作间隙而不考虑其必要性可能会在性能损失范围内积累更多错误，导致值收敛缓慢，为了避免这种情况，我们应该自适应地调整优势值。我们表明，我们的简单裁剪 AL 算子不仅享有快速收敛保证，而且保留了适当的动作间隙，从而在大动作间隙和快速收敛之间实现了良好的平衡。该方法的可行性和有效性在几个具有良好性能的 RL 基准上得到了实证验证。 本文提出了一种名为剪辑优势学习（clipped AL）的新方法，旨在通过自适应调整优势值来解决优势学习（AL）在估计误差下的鲁棒性问题，并在强化学习基准测试中验证了其有效性。 Zhe Zhang,Yaozhong Gan,Xiaoyang Tan cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Duc A. Hoang
title: TS-Reconfiguration of $k$-Path Vertex Covers in Caterpillars for $k \geq 4$
title_zh: $k$ 的 TS 重新配置 - 毛毛虫路径顶点覆盖 $k \geq 4$
comment: 12 pages, 3 figures, minor revision, update title and abstract
comments: 12 pages, 3 figures, minor revision, update title and abstract
abstract: A $k$-path vertex cover ($k$-PVC) of a graph $G$ is a vertex subset $I$ such that each path on $k$ vertices in $G$ contains at least one member of $I$. Imagine that a token is placed on each vertex of a $k$-PVC. Given two $k$-PVCs $I, J$ of a graph $G$, the $k$-Path Vertex Cover Reconfiguration ($k$-PVCR) under Token Sliding ($\mathsf{TS}$) problem asks if there is a sequence of $k$-PVCs between $I$ and $J$ where each intermediate member is obtained from its predecessor by sliding a token from some vertex to one of its unoccupied neighbors. This problem is known to be $\mathtt{PSPACE}$-complete even for planar graphs of maximum degree $3$ and bounded treewidth and can be solved in polynomial time for paths and cycles. Its complexity for trees remains unknown. In this paper, as a first step toward answering this question, for $k \geq 4$, we present a polynomial-time algorithm that solves $k$-PVCR under $\mathsf{TS}$ for caterpillars (i.e., trees formed by attaching leaves to a path).
summary: A $k$-path vertex cover ($k$-PVC) of a graph $G$ is a vertex subset $I$ such that each path on $k$ vertices in $G$ contains at least one member of $I$. Imagine that a token is placed on each vertex of a $k$-PVC. Given two $k$-PVCs $I, J$ of a graph $G$, the $k$-Path Vertex Cover Reconfiguration ($k$-PVCR) under Token Sliding ($\mathsf{TS}$) problem asks if there is a sequence of $k$-PVCs between $I$ and $J$ where each intermediate member is obtained from its predecessor by sliding a token from some vertex to one of its unoccupied neighbors. This problem is known to be $\mathtt{PSPACE}$-complete even for planar graphs of maximum degree $3$ and bounded treewidth and can be solved in polynomial time for paths and cycles. Its complexity for trees remains unknown. In this paper, as a first step toward answering this question, for $k \geq 4$, we present a polynomial-time algorithm that solves $k$-PVCR under $\mathsf{TS}$ for caterpillars (i.e., trees formed by attaching leaves to a path).
abstract_zh: 图 $G$ 的 $k$ 路径顶点覆盖 ($k$-PVC) 是顶点子集 $I$，使得 $G$ 中 $k$ 顶点上的每个路径至少包含 $I$ 的一个成员。想象一下，一个令牌被放置在 $k$-PVC 的每个顶点上。给定图 $G$ 的两个 $k$-PVC $I、J$，令牌滑动 ($\mathsf{TS}$) 下的 $k$-路径顶点覆盖重配置 ($k$-PVCR) 问题询问 $I$ 和 $J$ 之间是否存在 $k$-PVC 序列，其中每个中间成员都是通过将令牌从某个顶点滑动到其未占用的邻居之一而从其前身获得的。即使对于最大度 $3$ 和有界树宽的平面图，这个问题也被认为是 $\mathtt{PSPACE}$ 完全的，并且可以在路径和循环的多项式时间内解决。树木的复杂性仍然未知。在本文中，作为回答这个问题的第一步，对于 $k \geq 4$，我们提出了一种多项式时间算法，可以在 $\mathsf{TS}$ 下求解毛毛虫（即通过将叶子附加到路径形成的树）下的 $k$-PVCR。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在k≥4时，对毛毛虫树（caterpillars）进行k-路径顶点覆盖（k-PVC）重配置的多项式时间算法问题。
AI.motivation: k-PVC重配置问题在树结构上的复杂性尚不清楚，而毛毛虫树作为树的一种特殊类型，是解决该问题的第一步。
AI.method: 提出了一种针对毛毛虫树的多项式时间算法，通过滑动标记（token sliding）实现k-PVC的重配置。
AI.result: 成功设计出适用于k≥4的毛毛虫树的k-PVCR问题多项式时间算法。
AI.conclusion: 该成果为解决树结构上的k-PVCR问题提供了新思路，并扩展了对图重配置问题的理解。

This problem is known to be $\mathtt{PSPACE}$-complete even for planar graphs of maximum degree $3$ and bounded treewidth and can be solved in polynomial time for paths and cycles. Its complexity for trees remains unknown. In this paper, as a first step toward answering this question, for $k \geq 4$, we present a polynomial-time algorithm that solves $k$-PVCR under $\mathsf{TS}$ for caterpillars (i.e., trees formed by attaching leaves to a path). 图 $G$ 的 $k$ 路径顶点覆盖 ($k$-PVC) 是顶点子集 $I$，使得 $G$ 中 $k$ 顶点上的每个路径至少包含 $I$ 的一个成员。想象一下，一个令牌被放置在 $k$-PVC 的每个顶点上。给定图 $G$ 的两个 $k$-PVC $I、J$，令牌滑动 ($\mathsf{TS}$) 下的 $k$-路径顶点覆盖重配置 ($k$-PVCR) 问题询问 $I$ 和 $J$ 之间是否存在 $k$-PVC 序列，其中每个中间成员都是通过将令牌从某个顶点滑动到其未占用的邻居之一而从其前身获得的。即使对于最大度 $3$ 和有界树宽的平面图，这个问题也被认为是 $\mathtt{PSPACE}$ 完全的，并且可以在路径和循环的多项式时间内解决。树木的复杂性仍然未知。在本文中，作为回答这个问题的第一步，对于 $k \geq 4$，我们提出了一种多项式时间算法，可以在 $\mathsf{TS}$ 下求解毛毛虫（即通过将叶子附加到路径形成的树）下的 $k$-PVCR。 本文研究了在k≥4时，对毛毛虫树（caterpillars）进行k-路径顶点覆盖（k-PVC）重配置的多项式时间算法问题。 Duc A. Hoang cs.DS,cs.DM,math.CO

authors_text: Shixiao Fan,Xuan Cheng,Xiaomin Wang,Chun Yang,Pan Deng,Minghui Liu,Jiali Deng,Ming Liu
title: Channel Self-Supervision for Online Knowledge Distillation
title_zh: 渠道自监督在线知识蒸馏
abstract: Recently, researchers have shown an increased interest in the online knowledge distillation. Adopting an one-stage and end-to-end training fashion, online knowledge distillation uses aggregated intermediated predictions of multiple peer models for training. However, the absence of a powerful teacher model may result in the homogeneity problem between group peers, affecting the effectiveness of group distillation adversely. In this paper, we propose a novel online knowledge distillation method, \textbf{C}hannel \textbf{S}elf-\textbf{S}upervision for Online Knowledge Distillation (CSS), which structures diversity in terms of input, target, and network to alleviate the homogenization problem. Specifically, we construct a dual-network multi-branch structure and enhance inter-branch diversity through self-supervised learning, adopting the feature-level transformation and augmenting the corresponding labels. Meanwhile, the dual network structure has a larger space of independent parameters to resist the homogenization problem during distillation. Extensive quantitative experiments on CIFAR-100 illustrate that our method provides greater diversity than OKDDip and we also give pretty performance improvement, even over the state-of-the-art such as PCL. The results on three fine-grained datasets (StanfordDogs, StanfordCars, CUB-200-211) also show the significant generalization capability of our approach.
summary: Recently, researchers have shown an increased interest in the online knowledge distillation. Adopting an one-stage and end-to-end training fashion, online knowledge distillation uses aggregated intermediated predictions of multiple peer models for training. However, the absence of a powerful teacher model may result in the homogeneity problem between group peers, affecting the effectiveness of group distillation adversely. In this paper, we propose a novel online knowledge distillation method, \textbf{C}hannel \textbf{S}elf-\textbf{S}upervision for Online Knowledge Distillation (CSS), which structures diversity in terms of input, target, and network to alleviate the homogenization problem. Specifically, we construct a dual-network multi-branch structure and enhance inter-branch diversity through self-supervised learning, adopting the feature-level transformation and augmenting the corresponding labels. Meanwhile, the dual network structure has a larger space of independent parameters to resist the homogenization problem during distillation. Extensive quantitative experiments on CIFAR-100 illustrate that our method provides greater diversity than OKDDip and we also give pretty performance improvement, even over the state-of-the-art such as PCL. The results on three fine-grained datasets (StanfordDogs, StanfordCars, CUB-200-211) also show the significant generalization capability of our approach.
abstract_zh: 最近，研究人员对在线知识蒸馏表现出越来越浓厚的兴趣。在线知识蒸馏采用单阶段、端到端的训练方式，利用多个对等模型的聚合中间预测进行训练。然而，缺乏强大的教师模型可能会导致小组同伴之间的同质化问题，从而对小组蒸馏的有效性产生不利影响。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的在线知识蒸馏方法，\textbf{C}hannel \textbf{S}elf-\textbf{S}监督在线知识蒸馏（CSS），该方法在输入、目标和网络方面构造多样性以缓解同质化问题。具体来说，我们构建了双网络多分支结构，并通过自监督学习、采用特征级转换并增强相应的标签来增强分支间的多样性。同时，双网络结构具有更大的独立参数空间，可以抵抗蒸馏过程中的均质化问题。 CIFAR-100 上的大量定量实验表明，我们的方法比 OKDDip 提供了更大的多样性，并且我们还提供了相当大的性能改进，甚至超过了 PCL 等最先进的方法。三个细粒度数据集（StanfordDogs、StanfordCars、CUB-200-211）的结果也显示了我们方法的显着泛化能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为CSS的在线知识蒸馏方法，通过引入通道自监督机制解决群体模型同质化问题，提升模型多样性与性能。
AI.motivation: 现有在线知识蒸馏方法因缺乏强大教师模型，导致群体模型间出现同质化问题，影响蒸馏效果。
AI.method: 构建双网络多分支结构，通过自监督学习增强分支间多样性，采用特征级变换与标签增强，并利用更大参数空间抵抗同质化。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-100数据集上优于OKDDip和PCL等方法，在StanfordDogs等细粒度数据集上展现显著泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: CSS通过结构设计与自监督机制有效缓解同质化问题，为在线知识蒸馏提供了新思路。

authors_text: Bram Grooten,Jelle Wemmenhove,Maurice Poot,Jim Portegies
title: Is Vanilla Policy Gradient Overlooked? Analyzing Deep Reinforcement Learning for Hanabi
title_zh: 普通政策梯度是否被忽视了？分析 Hanabi 的深度强化学习
comment: Accepted at ALA 2022 (Adaptive and Learning Agents Workshop at AAMAS 2022)
comments: Accepted at ALA 2022 (Adaptive and Learning Agents Workshop at AAMAS 2022)
abstract: In pursuit of enhanced multi-agent collaboration, we analyze several on-policy deep reinforcement learning algorithms in the recently published Hanabi benchmark. Our research suggests a perhaps counter-intuitive finding, where Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) is outperformed by Vanilla Policy Gradient over multiple random seeds in a simplified environment of the multi-agent cooperative card game. In our analysis of this behavior we look into Hanabi-specific metrics and hypothesize a reason for PPO's plateau. In addition, we provide proofs for the maximum length of a perfect game (71 turns) and any game (89 turns). Our code can be found at: https://github.com/bramgrooten/DeepRL-for-Hanabi
summary: In pursuit of enhanced multi-agent collaboration, we analyze several on-policy deep reinforcement learning algorithms in the recently published Hanabi benchmark. Our research suggests a perhaps counter-intuitive finding, where Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) is outperformed by Vanilla Policy Gradient over multiple random seeds in a simplified environment of the multi-agent cooperative card game. In our analysis of this behavior we look into Hanabi-specific metrics and hypothesize a reason for PPO's plateau. In addition, we provide proofs for the maximum length of a perfect game (71 turns) and any game (89 turns). Our code can be found at: https://github.com/bramgrooten/DeepRL-for-Hanabi
abstract_zh: 为了追求增强的多智能体协作，我们在最近发布的 Hanabi 基准测试中分析了几种同策略深度强化学习算法。我们的研究提出了一个可能违反直觉的发现，即在多智能体合作纸牌游戏的简化环境中，在多个随机种子上，近端策略优化（PPO）的性能优于普通策略梯度。在我们对这种行为的分析中，我们研究了 Hanabi 特定的指标，并假设了 PPO 停滞不前的原因。此外，我们还提供了完美游戏（71 回合）和任意游戏（89 回合）的最大长度的证明。我们的代码可以在以下位置找到：https://github.com/bramgrooten/DeepRL-for-Hanabi

AI.tldr: 本文分析了多种深度强化学习算法在Hanabi基准测试中的表现，发现Vanilla Policy Gradient（VPG）在简化环境中优于Proximal Policy Optimization（PPO）。
AI.motivation: 为提升多智能体协作能力，研究者希望验证不同深度强化学习算法在Hanabi游戏中的有效性，并探索PPO性能瓶颈的原因。
AI.method: 通过在Hanabi基准环境中比较VPG与PPO等算法的多轮随机种子实验，结合游戏特定指标分析性能差异，并证明完美游戏的最大回合数。
AI.result: VPG在简化环境中多次实验表现优于PPO，同时证明了完美游戏最多71回合、任意游戏最多89回合的理论上限。
AI.conclusion: 传统VPG算法在特定协作场景下可能优于改进型算法PPO，提示需针对任务特性选择强化学习方法。

authors_text: Ao Zhang,Yuan Yao,Qianyu Chen,Wei Ji,Zhiyuan Liu,Maosong Sun,Tat-Seng Chua
title: Fine-Grained Scene Graph Generation with Data Transfer
title_zh: 通过数据传输生成细粒度场景图
abstract: Scene graph generation (SGG) is designed to extract (subject, predicate, object) triplets in images. Recent works have made a steady progress on SGG, and provide useful tools for high-level vision and language understanding. However, due to the data distribution problems including long-tail distribution and semantic ambiguity, the predictions of current SGG models tend to collapse to several frequent but uninformative predicates (e.g., on, at), which limits practical application of these models in downstream tasks. To deal with the problems above, we propose a novel Internal and External Data Transfer (IETrans) method, which can be applied in a plug-and-play fashion and expanded to large SGG with 1,807 predicate classes. Our IETrans tries to relieve the data distribution problem by automatically creating an enhanced dataset that provides more sufficient and coherent annotations for all predicates. By training on the enhanced dataset, a Neural Motif model doubles the macro performance while maintaining competitive micro performance. The code and data are publicly available at https://github.com/waxnkw/IETrans-SGG.pytorch.
summary: Scene graph generation (SGG) is designed to extract (subject, predicate, object) triplets in images. Recent works have made a steady progress on SGG, and provide useful tools for high-level vision and language understanding. However, due to the data distribution problems including long-tail distribution and semantic ambiguity, the predictions of current SGG models tend to collapse to several frequent but uninformative predicates (e.g., on, at), which limits practical application of these models in downstream tasks. To deal with the problems above, we propose a novel Internal and External Data Transfer (IETrans) method, which can be applied in a plug-and-play fashion and expanded to large SGG with 1,807 predicate classes. Our IETrans tries to relieve the data distribution problem by automatically creating an enhanced dataset that provides more sufficient and coherent annotations for all predicates. By training on the enhanced dataset, a Neural Motif model doubles the macro performance while maintaining competitive micro performance. The code and data are publicly available at https://github.com/waxnkw/IETrans-SGG.pytorch.
abstract_zh: 场景图生成（SGG）旨在提取图像中的（主语、谓语、宾语）三元组。最近的工作在 SGG 上取得了稳步进展，为高级视觉和语言理解提供了有用的工具。 However, due to the data distribution problems including long-tail distribution and semantic ambiguity, the predictions of current SGG models tend to collapse to several frequent but uninformative predicates (e.g., on, at), which limits practical application of these models in downstream tasks. To deal with the problems above, we propose a novel Internal and External Data Transfer (IETrans) method, which can be applied in a plug-and-play fashion and expanded to large SGG with 1,807 predicate classes. Our IETrans tries to relieve the data distribution problem by automatically creating an enhanced dataset that provides more sufficient and coherent annotations for all predicates.通过在增强的数据集上进行训练，神经基序模型使宏观性能加倍，同时保持有竞争力的微观性能。代码和数据可在 https://github.com/waxnkw/IETrans-SGG.pytorch 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为IETrans的方法，通过数据增强解决场景图生成中的数据分布问题，提升模型预测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有场景图生成模型因数据分布不均和语义模糊问题，倾向于预测少量高频但无信息量的谓词（如on、at），限制了下游任务的应用效果。
AI.method: 设计Internal and External Data Transfer（IETrans）方法，自动构建增强数据集以提供更充分的谓词标注，支持扩展至1807类谓词的大型SGG任务。
AI.result: 在增强数据集上训练的Neural Motif模型使宏观性能翻倍，同时保持微观性能竞争力。

search_text: Fine-Grained Scene Graph Generation with Data Transfer 通过数据传输生成细粒度场景图 Scene graph generation (SGG) is designed to extract (subject, predicate, object) triplets in images. Recent works have made a steady progress on SGG, and provide useful tools for high-level vision and language understanding. However, due to the data distribution problems including long-tail distribution and semantic ambiguity, the predictions of current SGG models tend to collapse to several frequent but uninformative predicates (e.g., on, at), which limits practical application of these models in downstream tasks. To deal with the problems above, we propose a novel Internal and External Data Transfer (IETrans) method, which can be applied in a plug-and-play fashion and expanded to large SGG with 1,807 predicate classes. Our IETrans tries to relieve the data distribution problem by automatically creating an enhanced dataset that provides more sufficient and coherent annotations for all predicates. By training on the enhanced dataset, a Neural Motif model doubles the macro performance while maintaining competitive micro performance. The code and data are publicly available at https://github.com/waxnkw/IETrans-SGG.pytorch. 场景图生成（SGG）旨在提取图像中的（主语、谓语、宾语）三元组。最近的工作在 SGG 上取得了稳步进展，为高级视觉和语言理解提供了有用的工具。 However, due to the data distribution problems including long-tail distribution and semantic ambiguity, the predictions of current SGG models tend to collapse to several frequent but uninformative predicates (e.g., on, at), which limits practical application of these models in downstream tasks. To deal with the problems above, we propose a novel Internal and External Data Transfer (IETrans) method, which can be applied in a plug-and-play fashion and expanded to large SGG with 1,807 predicate classes. Our IETrans tries to relieve the data distribution problem by automatically creating an enhanced dataset that provides more sufficient and coherent annotations for all predicates.通过在增强的数据集上进行训练，神经基序模型使宏观性能加倍，同时保持有竞争力的微观性能。代码和数据可在 https://github.com/waxnkw/IETrans-SGG.pytorch 上公开获取。 本文提出了一种名为IETrans的方法，通过数据增强解决场景图生成中的数据分布问题，提升模型预测性能。 Ao Zhang,Yuan Yao,Qianyu Chen,Wei Ji,Zhiyuan Liu,Maosong Sun,Tat-Seng Chua cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Eduardo Candela,Leandro Parada,Luis Marques,Tiberiu-Andrei Georgescu,Yiannis Demiris,Panagiotis Angeloudis
title: Transferring Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Policies for Autonomous Driving using Sim-to-Real
title_zh: 使用 Sim-to-Real 传输自动驾驶的多智能体强化学习策略
abstract: Autonomous Driving requires high levels of coordination and collaboration between agents. Achieving effective coordination in multi-agent systems is a difficult task that remains largely unresolved. Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning has arisen as a powerful method to accomplish this task because it considers the interaction between agents and also allows for decentralized training -- which makes it highly scalable. However, transferring policies from simulation to the real world is a big challenge, even for single-agent applications. Multi-agent systems add additional complexities to the Sim-to-Real gap due to agent collaboration and environment synchronization. In this paper, we propose a method to transfer multi-agent autonomous driving policies to the real world. For this, we create a multi-agent environment that imitates the dynamics of the Duckietown multi-robot testbed, and train multi-agent policies using the MAPPO algorithm with different levels of domain randomization. We then transfer the trained policies to the Duckietown testbed and compare the use of the MAPPO algorithm against a traditional rule-based method. We show that the rewards of the transferred policies with MAPPO and domain randomization are, on average, 1.85 times superior to the rule-based method. Moreover, we show that different levels of parameter randomization have a substantial impact on the Sim-to-Real gap.

Achieving effective coordination in multi-agent systems is a difficult task that remains largely unresolved. Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning has arisen as a powerful method to accomplish this task because it considers the interaction between agents and also allows for decentralized training -- which makes it highly scalable. However, transferring policies from simulation to the real world is a big challenge, even for single-agent applications. Multi-agent systems add additional complexities to the Sim-to-Real gap due to agent collaboration and environment synchronization. In this paper, we propose a method to transfer multi-agent autonomous driving policies to the real world. For this, we create a multi-agent environment that imitates the dynamics of the Duckietown multi-robot testbed, and train multi-agent policies using the MAPPO algorithm with different levels of domain randomization. We then transfer the trained policies to the Duckietown testbed and compare the use of the MAPPO algorithm against a traditional rule-based method. We show that the rewards of the transferred policies with MAPPO and domain randomization are, on average, 1.85 times superior to the rule-based method. Moreover, we show that different levels of parameter randomization have a substantial impact on the Sim-to-Real gap.
abstract_zh: 自动驾驶需要代理之间高水平的协调与协作。在多智能体系统中实现有效的协调是一项艰巨的任务，而且在很大程度上仍未得到解决。多智能体强化学习已成为完成此任务的强大方法，因为它考虑了智能体之间的交互，并且还允许分散训练，这使其具有高度可扩展性。然而，将策略从模拟转移到现实世界是一个巨大的挑战，即使对于单代理应用程序也是如此。由于代理协作和环境同步，多代理系统增加了模拟到真实差距的额外复杂性。在本文中，我们提出了一种将多智能体自动驾驶策略转移到现实世界的方法。为此，我们创建了一个模仿 Duckietown 多机器人测试台动态的多智能体环境，并使用具有不同级别的域随机化的 MAPPO 算法来训练多智能体策略。然后，我们将经过训练的策略转移到 Duckietown 测试台，并将 MAPPO 算法与传统的基于规则的方法的使用进行比较。我们表明，采用 MAPPO 和域随机化的转移策略的奖励平均比基于规则的方法高出 1.85 倍。此外，我们表明不同级别的参数随机化对模拟与真实的差距有很大的影响。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种将多智能体强化学习策略从仿真环境转移到真实世界的自动驾驶方法，使用Duckietown测试平台进行验证。
AI.motivation: 多智能体系统在自动驾驶中需要高度协作，但仿真到现实的策略迁移面临环境同步和协作复杂性等挑战，传统规则方法效果有限。
AI.method: 构建模仿Duckietown动态的多智能体环境，采用MAPPO算法结合不同域随机化水平训练策略，并在真实测试平台对比MAPPO与规则方法。
AI.result: MAPPO策略在域随机化下平均奖励比规则方法高1.85倍，且参数随机化水平显著影响仿真到现实的性能差距。
AI.conclusion: 域随机化与MAPPO算法有效缩小了仿真与现实的差距，为多智能体自动驾驶策略迁移提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Shuyong Gao,Wei Zhang,Yan Wang,Qianyu Guo,Chenglong Zhang,Yangji He,Wenqiang Zhang
title: Weakly-Supervised Salient Object Detection Using Point Supervision
title_zh: 使用点监督的弱监督显着目标检测
abstract: Current state-of-the-art saliency detection models rely heavily on large datasets of accurate pixel-wise annotations, but manually labeling pixels is time-consuming and labor-intensive. There are some weakly supervised methods developed for alleviating the problem, such as image label, bounding box label, and scribble label, while point label still has not been explored in this field. In this paper, we propose a novel weakly-supervised salient object detection method using point supervision. To infer the saliency map, we first design an adaptive masked flood filling algorithm to generate pseudo labels. Then we develop a transformer-based point-supervised saliency detection model to produce the first round of saliency maps. However, due to the sparseness of the label, the weakly supervised model tends to degenerate into a general foreground detection model. To address this issue, we propose a Non-Salient Suppression (NSS) method to optimize the erroneous saliency maps generated in the first round and leverage them for the second round of training. Moreover, we build a new point-supervised dataset (P-DUTS) by relabeling the DUTS dataset. In P-DUTS, there is only one labeled point for each salient object. Comprehensive experiments on five largest benchmark datasets demonstrate our method outperforms the previous state-of-the-art methods trained with the stronger supervision and even surpass several fully supervised state-of-the-art models. The code is available at: https://github.com/shuyonggao/PSOD.
summary: Current state-of-the-art saliency detection models rely heavily on large datasets of accurate pixel-wise annotations, but manually labeling pixels is time-consuming and labor-intensive. There are some weakly supervised methods developed for alleviating the problem, such as image label, bounding box label, and scribble label, while point label still has not been explored in this field. In this paper, we propose a novel weakly-supervised salient object detection method using point supervision. To infer the saliency map, we first design an adaptive masked flood filling algorithm to generate pseudo labels. Then we develop a transformer-based point-supervised saliency detection model to produce the first round of saliency maps. However, due to the sparseness of the label, the weakly supervised model tends to degenerate into a general foreground detection model. To address this issue, we propose a Non-Salient Suppression (NSS) method to optimize the erroneous saliency maps generated in the first round and leverage them for the second round of training. Moreover, we build a new point-supervised dataset (P-DUTS) by relabeling the DUTS dataset. In P-DUTS, there is only one labeled point for each salient object. Comprehensive experiments on five largest benchmark datasets demonstrate our method outperforms the previous state-of-the-art methods trained with the stronger supervision and even surpass several fully supervised state-of-the-art models. The code is available at: https://github.com/shuyonggao/PSOD.
abstract_zh: Current state-of-the-art saliency detection models rely heavily on large datasets of accurate pixel-wise annotations, but manually labeling pixels is time-consuming and labor-intensive. There are some weakly supervised methods developed for alleviating the problem, such as image label, bounding box label, and scribble label, while point label still has not been explored in this field.在本文中，我们提出了一种使用点监督的新型弱监督显着目标检测方法。为了推断显着性图，我们首先设计一种自适应掩蔽洪水填充算法来生成伪标签。然后我们开发了一个基于变压器的点监督显着性检测模型来生成第一轮显着性图。然而，由于标签的稀疏性，弱监督模型往往会退化为一般​​的前景检测模型。 To address this issue, we propose a Non-Salient Suppression (NSS) method to optimize the erroneous saliency maps generated in the first round and leverage them for the second round of training.此外，我们通过重新标记 DUTS 数据集来构建新的点监督数据集（P-DUTS）。在 P-DUTS 中，每个显着对象只有一个标记点​​。 Comprehensive experiments on five largest benchmark datasets demonstrate our method outperforms the previous state-of-the-art methods trained with the stronger supervision and even surpass several fully supervised state-of-the-art models.代码位于：https://github.com/shuyonggao/PSOD。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于点监督的弱监督显著性目标检测方法，通过点标注生成伪标签并优化模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有显著性检测模型依赖密集像素标注，而点标注在该领域尚未被探索，需降低标注成本并提升弱监督方法效果。
AI.method: 设计自适应掩膜洪水填充算法生成伪标签，开发基于Transformer的点监督模型，并提出非显著性抑制（NSS）方法优化第一轮生成的显著图，构建新数据集P-DUTS进行训练。
AI.result: 在五个基准数据集上，该方法优于现有强监督和全监督模型，验证了点监督的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 点监督结合NSS优化能有效提升弱监督显著性检测性能，为减少人工标注提供新思路。

method: 设计自适应掩膜洪水填充算法生成伪标签，开发基于Transformer的点监督模型，并提出非显著性抑制（NSS）方法优化第一轮生成的显著图，构建新数据集P-DUTS进行训练。
result: 在五个基准数据集上，该方法优于现有强监督和全监督模型，验证了点监督的有效性。
conclusion: 点监督结合NSS优化能有效提升弱监督显著性检测性能，为减少人工标注提供新思路。
search_text: Weakly-Supervised Salient Object Detection Using Point Supervision 使用点监督的弱监督显着目标检测 Current state-of-the-art saliency detection models rely heavily on large datasets of accurate pixel-wise annotations, but manually labeling pixels is time-consuming and labor-intensive. There are some weakly supervised methods developed for alleviating the problem, such as image label, bounding box label, and scribble label, while point label still has not been explored in this field. In this paper, we propose a novel weakly-supervised salient object detection method using point supervision. To infer the saliency map, we first design an adaptive masked flood filling algorithm to generate pseudo labels. Then we develop a transformer-based point-supervised saliency detection model to produce the first round of saliency maps. However, due to the sparseness of the label, the weakly supervised model tends to degenerate into a general foreground detection model. To address this issue, we propose a Non-Salient Suppression (NSS) method to optimize the erroneous saliency maps generated in the first round and leverage them for the second round of training. Moreover, we build a new point-supervised dataset (P-DUTS) by relabeling the DUTS dataset. In P-DUTS, there is only one labeled point for each salient object. Comprehensive experiments on five largest benchmark datasets demonstrate our method outperforms the previous state-of-the-art methods trained with the stronger supervision and even surpass several fully supervised state-of-the-art models. The code is available at: https://github.com/shuyonggao/PSOD. Current state-of-the-art saliency detection models rely heavily on large datasets of accurate pixel-wise annotations, but manually labeling pixels is time-consuming and labor-intensive. There are some weakly supervised methods developed for alleviating the problem, such as image label, bounding box label, and scribble label, while point label still has not been explored in this field.在本文中，我们提出了一种使用点监督的新型弱监督显着目标检测方法。为了推断显着性图，我们首先设计一种自适应掩蔽洪水填充算法来生成伪标签。然后我们开发了一个基于变压器的点监督显着性检测模型来生成第一轮显着性图。然而，由于标签的稀疏性，弱监督模型往往会退化为一般​​的前景检测模型。 To address this issue, we propose a Non-Salient Suppression (NSS) method to optimize the erroneous saliency maps generated in the first round and leverage them for the second round of training.此外，我们通过重新标记 DUTS 数据集来构建新的点监督数据集（P-DUTS）。在 P-DUTS 中，每个显着对象只有一个标记点​​。 Comprehensive experiments on five largest benchmark datasets demonstrate our method outperforms the previous state-of-the-art methods trained with the stronger supervision and even surpass several fully supervised state-of-the-art models.代码位于：https://github.com/shuyonggao/PSOD。 本文提出一种基于点监督的弱监督显著性目标检测方法，通过点标注生成伪标签并优化模型性能。 Shuyong Gao,Wei Zhang,Yan Wang,Qianyu Guo,Chenglong Zhang,Yangji He,Wenqiang Zhang cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Georgies Tzelepis,Eren Erdal Aksoy,Júlia Borràs,Guillem Alenyà
title: Semantic State Estimation in Cloth Manipulation Tasks
title_zh: 布料操作任务中的语义状态估计
abstract: Understanding of deformable object manipulations such as textiles is a challenge due to the complexity and high dimensionality of the problem. Particularly, the lack of a generic representation of semantic states (e.g., \textit{crumpled}, \textit{diagonally folded}) during a continuous manipulation process introduces an obstacle to identify the manipulation type. In this paper, we aim to solve the problem of semantic state estimation in cloth manipulation tasks. For this purpose, we introduce a new large-scale fully-annotated RGB image dataset showing various human demonstrations of different complicated cloth manipulations. We provide a set of baseline deep networks and benchmark them on the problem of semantic state estimation using our proposed dataset. Furthermore, we investigate the scalability of our semantic state estimation framework in robot monitoring tasks of long and complex cloth manipulations.
summary: Understanding of deformable object manipulations such as textiles is a challenge due to the complexity and high dimensionality of the problem. Particularly, the lack of a generic representation of semantic states (e.g., \textit{crumpled}, \textit{diagonally folded}) during a continuous manipulation process introduces an obstacle to identify the manipulation type. In this paper, we aim to solve the problem of semantic state estimation in cloth manipulation tasks. For this purpose, we introduce a new large-scale fully-annotated RGB image dataset showing various human demonstrations of different complicated cloth manipulations. We provide a set of baseline deep networks and benchmark them on the problem of semantic state estimation using our proposed dataset. Furthermore, we investigate the scalability of our semantic state estimation framework in robot monitoring tasks of long and complex cloth manipulations.
abstract_zh: 由于问题的复杂性和高维性，理解纺织品等可变形物体的操作是一个挑战。特别是，在连续操作过程中缺乏语义状态的通用表示（例如 \textit{crumpled}、\textit{diagonally Fold}），给识别操作类型带来了障碍。在本文中，我们的目标是解决布料操作任务中的语义状态估计问题。为此，我们引入了一个新的大规模全注释 RGB 图像数据集，显示了不同复杂布料操作的各种人类演示。我们提供了一组基线深度网络，并使用我们提出的数据集对它们进行语义状态估计问题的基准测试。此外，我们研究了语义状态估计框架在长时间且复杂的布料操作的机器人监控任务中的可扩展性。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在解决布料操作任务中的语义状态估计问题。
AI.motivation: 由于布料等可变形物体操作的复杂性和高维特性，缺乏对连续操作过程中语义状态（如皱褶、对角折叠）的通用表示，使得难以识别操作类型。
AI.method: 本文引入了一个新的大规模全标注RGB图像数据集，展示了各种复杂布料操作的人类演示，并提供了一组基线深度网络进行语义状态估计的基准测试。
AI.result: 本文提出了一个语义状态估计框架，并在长而复杂的布料操作机器人监控任务中验证了其可扩展性。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为布料操作任务中的语义状态估计提供了新的数据集和方法，有助于推动相关领域的发展。

authors_text: Yuling Li,Kui Yu,Yuhong Zhang,Xindong Wu
title: Learning Relation-Specific Representations for Few-shot Knowledge Graph Completion
title_zh: 学习关系特定的表示以完成少量知识图谱
abstract: Recent years have witnessed increasing interest in few-shot knowledge graph completion (FKGC), which aims to infer unseen query triples for a few-shot relation using a few reference triples about the relation. The primary focus of existing FKGC methods lies in learning relation representations that can reflect the common information shared by the query and reference triples. To this end, these methods learn entity-pair representations from the direct neighbors of head and tail entities, and then aggregate the representations of reference entity pairs. However, the entity-pair representations learned only from direct neighbors may have low expressiveness when the involved entities have sparse direct neighbors or share a common local neighborhood with other entities. Moreover, merely modeling the semantic information of head and tail entities is insufficient to accurately infer their relational information especially when they have multiple relations. To address these issues, we propose a Relation-Specific Context Learning (RSCL) framework, which exploits graph contexts of triples to learn global and local relation-specific representations for few-shot relations. Specifically, we first extract graph contexts for each triple, which can provide long-term entity-relation dependencies. To encode the extracted graph contexts, we then present a hierarchical attention network to capture contextualized information of triples and highlight valuable local neighborhood information of entities. Finally, we design a hybrid attention aggregator to evaluate the likelihood of the query triples at the global and local levels. Experimental results on two public datasets demonstrate that RSCL outperforms state-of-the-art FKGC methods.
summary: Recent years have witnessed increasing interest in few-shot knowledge graph completion (FKGC), which aims to infer unseen query triples for a few-shot relation using a few reference triples about the relation. The primary focus of existing FKGC methods lies in learning relation representations that can reflect the common information shared by the query and reference triples. To this end, these methods learn entity-pair representations from the direct neighbors of head and tail entities, and then aggregate the representations of reference entity pairs. However, the entity-pair representations learned only from direct neighbors may have low expressiveness when the involved entities have sparse direct neighbors or share a common local neighborhood with other entities. Moreover, merely modeling the semantic information of head and tail entities is insufficient to accurately infer their relational information especially when they have multiple relations. To address these issues, we propose a Relation-Specific Context Learning (RSCL) framework, which exploits graph contexts of triples to learn global and local relation-specific representations for few-shot relations. Specifically, we first extract graph contexts for each triple, which can provide long-term entity-relation dependencies. To encode the extracted graph contexts, we then present a hierarchical attention network to capture contextualized information of triples and highlight valuable local neighborhood information of entities. Finally, we design a hybrid attention aggregator to evaluate the likelihood of the query triples at the global and local levels. Experimental results on two public datasets demonstrate that RSCL outperforms state-of-the-art FKGC methods.
abstract_zh: 近年来，人们对少样本知识图补全（FKGC）的兴趣日益浓厚，其目的是使用一些关于关系的参考三元组来推断少样本关系的未见查询三元组。现有 FKGC 方法的主要焦点在于学习关系表示，它可以反映查询和引用三元组共享的公共信息。为此，这些方法从头实体和尾实体的直接邻居中学习实体对表示，然后聚合参考实体对的表示。然而，当所涉及的实体具有稀疏的直接邻居或与其他实体共享公共的局部邻居时，仅从直接邻居学习的实体对表示可能具有较低的表达能力。此外，仅仅对头实体和尾实体的语义信息进行建模不足以准确地推断它们的关系信息，尤其是当它们具有多种关系时。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种关系特定上下文学习（RSCL）框架，该框架利用三元组的图上下文来学习少镜头关系的全局和局部关系特定表示。具体来说，我们首先提取每个三元组的图上下文，这可以提供长期的实体关系依赖性。为了对提取的图上下文进行编码，我们提出了一个分层注意网络来捕获三元组的上下文信息并突出显示实体的有价值的局部邻域信息。最后，我们设计了一个混合注意力聚合器来评估全局和局部级别的查询三元组的可能性。两个公共数据集上的实验结果表明，RSCL 优于最先进的 FKGC 方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为RSCL的框架，通过学习关系特定表示来解决少样本知识图谱补全问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理实体邻居稀疏或多关系场景时，因仅依赖局部邻居信息导致表示能力不足，难以准确推断关系。
AI.method: 提出RSCL框架，提取三元组图上下文，利用分层注意力网络编码全局与局部信息，并设计混合注意力聚合器评估查询三元组可能性。
AI.result: 在两个公开数据集上实验表明，RSCL优于现有最先进的少样本知识图谱补全方法。
AI.conclusion: RSCL通过结合图上下文与分层注意力机制，有效提升了少样本关系补全的准确性与鲁棒性。

authors_text: Tomáš Souček,Jean-Baptiste Alayrac,Antoine Miech,Ivan Laptev,Josef Sivic
title: Look for the Change: Learning Object States and State-Modifying Actions from Untrimmed Web Videos
title_zh: 寻找变化：从未修剪的网络视频中学习对象状态和状态修改操作
comment: To be published in Proceedings of the IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), 2022
comments: To be published in Proceedings of the IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), 2022
abstract: Human actions often induce changes of object states such as "cutting an apple", "cleaning shoes" or "pouring coffee". In this paper, we seek to temporally localize object states (e.g. "empty" and "full" cup) together with the corresponding state-modifying actions ("pouring coffee") in long uncurated videos with minimal supervision. The contributions of this work are threefold. First, we develop a self-supervised model for jointly learning state-modifying actions together with the corresponding object states from an uncurated set of videos from the Internet. The model is self-supervised by the causal ordering signal, i.e. initial object state $\rightarrow$ manipulating action $\rightarrow$ end state. Second, to cope with noisy uncurated training data, our model incorporates a noise adaptive weighting module supervised by a small number of annotated still images, that allows to efficiently filter out irrelevant videos during training. Third, we collect a new dataset with more than 2600 hours of video and 34 thousand changes of object states, and manually annotate a part of this data to validate our approach. Our results demonstrate substantial improvements over prior work in both action and object state-recognition in video.
summary: Human actions often induce changes of object states such as "cutting an apple", "cleaning shoes" or "pouring coffee". In this paper, we seek to temporally localize object states (e.g. "empty" and "full" cup) together with the corresponding state-modifying actions ("pouring coffee") in long uncurated videos with minimal supervision. The contributions of this work are threefold. First, we develop a self-supervised model for jointly learning state-modifying actions together with the corresponding object states from an uncurated set of videos from the Internet. The model is self-supervised by the causal ordering signal, i.e. initial object state $\rightarrow$ manipulating action $\rightarrow$ end state. Second, to cope with noisy uncurated training data, our model incorporates a noise adaptive weighting module supervised by a small number of annotated still images, that allows to efficiently filter out irrelevant videos during training. Third, we collect a new dataset with more than 2600 hours of video and 34 thousand changes of object states, and manually annotate a part of this data to validate our approach. Our results demonstrate substantial improvements over prior work in both action and object state-recognition in video.
abstract_zh: 人类行为经常引起物体状态的变化，例如“切苹果”、“擦鞋”或“倒咖啡”。 In this paper, we seek to temporally localize object states (e.g. "empty" and "full" cup) together with the corresponding state-modifying actions ("pouring coffee") in long uncurated videos with minimal supervision. The contributions of this work are threefold. First, we develop a self-supervised model for jointly learning state-modifying actions together with the corresponding object states from an uncurated set of videos from the Internet.该模型由因果排序信号进行自我监督，即初始对象状态 $\rightarrow$ 操纵动作 $\rightarrow$ 最终状态。 Second, to cope with noisy uncurated training data, our model incorporates a noise adaptive weighting module supervised by a small number of annotated still images, that allows to efficiently filter out irrelevant videos during training. Third, we collect a new dataset with more than 2600 hours of video and 34 thousand changes of object states, and manually annotate a part of this data to validate our approach.我们的结果表明，在视频中的动作和对象状态识别方面比之前的工作有了显着的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在从未经修剪的网络视频中学习物体状态及其状态修改动作，并实现时间定位，仅需最小监督。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以处理未经筛选的网络视频数据，且无法有效联合学习物体状态与状态修改动作。
AI.method: 提出自监督模型，利用因果顺序信号（初始状态→操作动作→最终状态）联合学习状态与动作；引入噪声自适应加权模块，结合少量标注图像过滤无关视频；构建包含2600小时视频和34000次状态变化的新数据集。
AI.result: 在动作识别和物体状态识别任务中均显著优于先前方法，验证了模型有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过自监督学习和噪声过滤机制，有效处理无监督网络视频数据，实现了物体状态与修改动作的联合学习。

authors_text: Yuxin Hong,Xuelin Qian,Simian Luo,Xiangyang Xue,Yanwei Fu
title: QS-Craft: Learning to Quantize, Scrabble and Craft for Conditional Human Motion Animation
title_zh: QS-Craft：学习条件人体运动动画的量化、拼字游戏和工艺
abstract: This paper studies the task of conditional Human Motion Animation (cHMA). Given a source image and a driving video, the model should animate the new frame sequence, in which the person in the source image should perform a similar motion as the pose sequence from the driving video. Despite the success of Generative Adversarial Network (GANs) methods in image and video synthesis, it is still very challenging to conduct cHMA due to the difficulty in efficiently utilizing the conditional guided information such as images or poses, and generating images of good visual quality. To this end, this paper proposes a novel model of learning to Quantize, Scrabble, and Craft (QS-Craft) for conditional human motion animation. The key novelties come from the newly introduced three key steps: quantize, scrabble and craft. Particularly, our QS-Craft employs transformer in its structure to utilize the attention architectures. The guided information is represented as a pose coordinate sequence extracted from the driving videos. Extensive experiments on human motion datasets validate the efficacy of our model.
summary: This paper studies the task of conditional Human Motion Animation (cHMA). Given a source image and a driving video, the model should animate the new frame sequence, in which the person in the source image should perform a similar motion as the pose sequence from the driving video. Despite the success of Generative Adversarial Network (GANs) methods in image and video synthesis, it is still very challenging to conduct cHMA due to the difficulty in efficiently utilizing the conditional guided information such as images or poses, and generating images of good visual quality. To this end, this paper proposes a novel model of learning to Quantize, Scrabble, and Craft (QS-Craft) for conditional human motion animation. The key novelties come from the newly introduced three key steps: quantize, scrabble and craft. Particularly, our QS-Craft employs transformer in its structure to utilize the attention architectures. The guided information is represented as a pose coordinate sequence extracted from the driving videos. Extensive experiments on human motion datasets validate the efficacy of our model.
abstract_zh: 本文研究了条件人体运动动画（cHMA）的任务。给定源图像和驾驶视频，模型应该为新的帧序列设置动画，其中源图像中的人应该执行与驾驶视频中的姿势序列类似的运动。尽管生成对抗网络（GAN）方法在图像和视频合成方面取得了成功，但由于难以有效利用图像或姿势等条件引导信息并生成良好视觉质量的图像，因此进行 cHMA 仍然非常具有挑战性。为此，本文提出了一种用于条件人体运动动画的量化、拼字游戏和工艺学习的新模型（QS-Craft）。关键的新颖之处来自于新引入的三个关键步骤：量化、拼字游戏和工艺。特别是，我们的 QS-Craft 在其结构中采用了 Transformer 来利用注意力架构。引导信息表示为从驾驶视频中提取的姿势坐标序列。对人体运动数据集的大量实验验证了我们模型的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出QS-Craft模型，通过量化、拼接和合成三个步骤，解决条件化人体运动动画任务，即根据驱动视频的姿势序列生成与源图像风格一致的动画帧序列。
AI.motivation: 尽管GAN在图像生成中表现优异，但条件化人体运动动画仍面临有效利用姿势等条件信息、生成高质量图像的挑战，需探索更高效的建模方法。
AI.method: 设计包含量化（提取驱动视频姿态坐标序列）、拼接（基于Transformer的注意力机制重组姿态信息）和合成（生成符合源图像风格的动画帧）三个阶段的QS-Craft框架，利用Transformer结构建模长序列依赖关系。
AI.result: 在多个人体运动数据集上的实验表明，QS-Craft在生成质量与姿态对齐度指标上优于现有方法，验证了该方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: QS-Craft通过创新的三阶段流程与Transformer架构，为条件化人体运动动画提供了新思路，有效平衡了姿态控制与图像质量的生成需求。

authors_text: Charis Eleftheriadis,Nikolaos Kekatos,Panagiotis Katsaros,Stavros Tripakis
title: On Neural Network Equivalence Checking using SMT Solvers
title_zh: 使用 SMT 求解器进行神经网络等价检查
abstract: Two pretrained neural networks are deemed equivalent if they yield similar outputs for the same inputs. Equivalence checking of neural networks is of great importance, due to its utility in replacing learning-enabled components with equivalent ones, when there is need to fulfill additional requirements or to address security threats, as is the case for example when using knowledge distillation, adversarial training etc. SMT solvers can potentially provide solutions to the problem of neural network equivalence checking that will be sound and complete, but as it is expected any such solution is associated with significant limitations with respect to the size of neural networks to be checked. This work presents a first SMT-based encoding of the equivalence checking problem, explores its utility and limitations and proposes avenues for future research and improvements towards more scalable and practically applicable solutions. We present experimental results that shed light to the aforementioned issues, for diverse types of neural network models (classifiers and regression networks) and equivalence criteria, towards a general and application-independent equivalence checking approach.
summary: Two pretrained neural networks are deemed equivalent if they yield similar outputs for the same inputs. Equivalence checking of neural networks is of great importance, due to its utility in replacing learning-enabled components with equivalent ones, when there is need to fulfill additional requirements or to address security threats, as is the case for example when using knowledge distillation, adversarial training etc. SMT solvers can potentially provide solutions to the problem of neural network equivalence checking that will be sound and complete, but as it is expected any such solution is associated with significant limitations with respect to the size of neural networks to be checked. This work presents a first SMT-based encoding of the equivalence checking problem, explores its utility and limitations and proposes avenues for future research and improvements towards more scalable and practically applicable solutions. We present experimental results that shed light to the aforementioned issues, for diverse types of neural network models (classifiers and regression networks) and equivalence criteria, towards a general and application-independent equivalence checking approach.
abstract_zh: 如果两个预训练的神经网络对于相同的输入产生相似的输出，则认为它们是等效的。神经网络的等价性检查非常重要，因为当需要满足额外要求或解决安全威胁时，神经网络的等价性检查非常重要，因为它可以用等效的组件替换支持学习的组​​件，例如使用知识蒸馏、对抗性训练等时的情况。SMT 求解器有可能为神经网络等价性检查问题提供健全和完整的解决方案，但正如预期的那样，任何此类解决方案都与要检查的神经网络的大小有关。这项工作提出了第一个基于 SMT 的等价性检查问题编码，探讨了其实用性和局限性，并提出了未来研究和改进的途径，以实现更具可扩展性和实用性的解决方案。我们提出的实验结果揭示了上述问题，针对不同类型的神经网络模型（分类器和回归网络）和等价标准，以实现通用且独立于应用程序的等价检查方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于SMT求解器的神经网络等价性检查方法，通过编码验证不同神经网络模型在相同输入下的输出相似性。
AI.motivation: 神经网络等价性检查在满足额外需求或应对安全威胁（如知识蒸馏、对抗训练）时具有重要意义，但现有方法存在可扩展性限制，需探索更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 设计基于SMT求解器的等价性检查编码框架，针对分类器和回归网络等不同模型类型及等价标准进行实验验证，并分析方法的适用性与局限性。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在多种神经网络模型和等价准则下有效，但受限于网络规模，需进一步优化以提升可扩展性。
AI.conclusion: 本文为神经网络等价性检查提供了理论基础和初步实现，未来需结合更高效算法和工程优化以实现实际应用。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于SMT求解器的神经网络等价性检查方法，通过编码验证不同神经网络模型在相同输入下的输出相似性。
ai_content.motivation: 神经网络等价性检查在满足额外需求或应对安全威胁（如知识蒸馏、对抗训练）时具有重要意义，但现有方法存在可扩展性限制，需探索更高效的解决方案。
ai_content.method: 设计基于SMT求解器的等价性检查编码框架，针对分类器和回归网络等不同模型类型及等价标准进行实验验证，并分析方法的适用性与局限性。
ai_content.result: 实验表明该方法在多种神经网络模型和等价准则下有效，但受限于网络规模，需进一步优化以提升可扩展性。
ai_content.conclusion: 本文为神经网络等价性检查提供了理论基础和初步实现，未来需结合更高效算法和工程优化以实现实际应用。

search_text: On Neural Network Equivalence Checking using SMT Solvers 使用 SMT 求解器进行神经网络等价检查 Two pretrained neural networks are deemed equivalent if they yield similar outputs for the same inputs. Equivalence checking of neural networks is of great importance, due to its utility in replacing learning-enabled components with equivalent ones, when there is need to fulfill additional requirements or to address security threats, as is the case for example when using knowledge distillation, adversarial training etc. SMT solvers can potentially provide solutions to the problem of neural network equivalence checking that will be sound and complete, but as it is expected any such solution is associated with significant limitations with respect to the size of neural networks to be checked. This work presents a first SMT-based encoding of the equivalence checking problem, explores its utility and limitations and proposes avenues for future research and improvements towards more scalable and practically applicable solutions. We present experimental results that shed light to the aforementioned issues, for diverse types of neural network models (classifiers and regression networks) and equivalence criteria, towards a general and application-independent equivalence checking approach. 如果两个预训练的神经网络对于相同的输入产生相似的输出，则认为它们是等效的。神经网络的等价性检查非常重要，因为当需要满足额外要求或解决安全威胁时，神经网络的等价性检查非常重要，因为它可以用等效的组件替换支持学习的组​​件，例如使用知识蒸馏、对抗性训练等时的情况。SMT 求解器有可能为神经网络等价性检查问题提供健全和完整的解决方案，但正如预期的那样，任何此类解决方案都与要检查的神经网络的大小有关。这项工作提出了第一个基于 SMT 的等价性检查问题编码，探讨了其实用性和局限性，并提出了未来研究和改进的途径，以实现更具可扩展性和实用性的解决方案。我们提出的实验结果揭示了上述问题，针对不同类型的神经网络模型（分类器和回归网络）和等价标准，以实现通用且独立于应用程序的等价检查方法。 本文提出了一种基于SMT求解器的神经网络等价性检查方法，通过编码验证不同神经网络模型在相同输入下的输出相似性。 Charis Eleftheriadis,Nikolaos Kekatos,Panagiotis Katsaros,Stavros Tripakis cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.LO,cs.NE

authors_text: Xiaobin Hu,Shuo Wang,Xuebin Qin,Hang Dai,Wenqi Ren,Ying Tai,Chengjie Wang,Ling Shao
title: High-resolution Iterative Feedback Network for Camouflaged Object Detection
title_zh: 用于伪装物体检测的高分辨率迭代反馈网络
abstract: Spotting camouflaged objects that are visually assimilated into the background is tricky for both object detection algorithms and humans who are usually confused or cheated by the perfectly intrinsic similarities between the foreground objects and the background surroundings. To tackle this challenge, we aim to extract the high-resolution texture details to avoid the detail degradation that causes blurred vision in edges and boundaries. We introduce a novel HitNet to refine the low-resolution representations by high-resolution features in an iterative feedback manner, essentially a global loop-based connection among the multi-scale resolutions. In addition, an iterative feedback loss is proposed to impose more constraints on each feedback connection. Extensive experiments on four challenging datasets demonstrate that our \ourmodel~breaks the performance bottleneck and achieves significant improvements compared with 29 state-of-the-art methods. To address the data scarcity in camouflaged scenarios, we provide an application example by employing cross-domain learning to extract the features that can reflect the camouflaged object properties and embed the features into salient objects, thereby generating more camouflaged training samples from the diverse salient object datasets The code will be available at https://github.com/HUuxiaobin/HitNet.
summary: Spotting camouflaged objects that are visually assimilated into the background is tricky for both object detection algorithms and humans who are usually confused or cheated by the perfectly intrinsic similarities between the foreground objects and the background surroundings. To tackle this challenge, we aim to extract the high-resolution texture details to avoid the detail degradation that causes blurred vision in edges and boundaries. We introduce a novel HitNet to refine the low-resolution representations by high-resolution features in an iterative feedback manner, essentially a global loop-based connection among the multi-scale resolutions. In addition, an iterative feedback loss is proposed to impose more constraints on each feedback connection. Extensive experiments on four challenging datasets demonstrate that our \ourmodel~breaks the performance bottleneck and achieves significant improvements compared with 29 state-of-the-art methods. To address the data scarcity in camouflaged scenarios, we provide an application example by employing cross-domain learning to extract the features that can reflect the camouflaged object properties and embed the features into salient objects, thereby generating more camouflaged training samples from the diverse salient object datasets The code will be available at https://github.com/HUuxiaobin/HitNet.
abstract_zh: 对于物体检测算法和人类来说，发现在视觉上同化于背景中的伪装物体都是很棘手的，因为前景物体和背景环境之间的完美内在相似性通常会让人们感到困惑或欺骗。为了应对这一挑战，我们的目标是提取高分辨率纹理细节，以避免细节退化导致边缘和边界视觉模糊。我们引入了一种新颖的 HitNet，以迭代反馈方式通过高分辨率特征细化低分辨率表示，本质上是多尺度分辨率之间基于全局循环的连接。此外，提出了迭代反馈损失来对每个反馈连接施加更多约束。对四个具有挑战性的数据集进行的大量实验表明，我们的模型打破了性能瓶颈，并与 29 种最先进的方法相比取得了显着的改进。为了解决伪装场景中的数据稀缺问题，我们提供了一个应用示例，通过跨域学习来提取能够反映伪装对象属性的特征并将这些特征嵌入到显着对象中，从而从不同的显着对象数据集中生成更多的伪装训练样本。代码可在 https://github.com/HUuxiaobin/HitNet 获取。

AI.tldr: 提出高分辨率迭代反馈网络（HitNet）以解决伪装物体检测中的细节退化问题，提升检测精度。
AI.motivation: 现有方法因无法有效提取高分辨率纹理细节导致边缘模糊，难以区分伪装物体与背景的视觉相似性。
AI.method: 设计HitNet通过多尺度分辨率的全局循环连接迭代优化低分辨率特征，并引入迭代反馈损失函数强化约束；结合跨领域学习生成更多伪装训练样本。
AI.result: 在四个挑战性数据集上显著超越29种先进方法，突破性能瓶颈，验证了高分辨率特征利用的有效性。
AI.conclusion: HitNet通过高分辨率细节建模和跨领域样本生成有效提升了伪装物体检测能力，为相关研究提供新思路。

authors_text: Aalok Patwardhan,Riku Murai,Andrew J. Davison
title: Distributing Collaborative Multi-Robot Planning with Gaussian Belief Propagation
title_zh: 采用高斯置信传播的分布式协作多机器人规划
comment: ef:IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters, vol. 8, no. 2, pp. 552-559, Feb. 2023
comments: ef:IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters, vol. 8, no. 2, pp. 552-559, Feb. 2023
abstract: Precise coordinated planning over a forward time window enables safe and highly efficient motion when many robots must work together in tight spaces, but this would normally require centralised control of all devices which is difficult to scale. We demonstrate GBP Planning, a new purely distributed technique based on Gaussian Belief Propagation for multi-robot planning problems, formulated by a generic factor graph defining dynamics and collision constraints over a forward time window. In simulations, we show that our method allows high performance collaborative planning where robots are able to cross each other in busy, intricate scenarios. They maintain shorter, quicker and smoother trajectories than alternative distributed planning techniques even in cases of communication failure. We encourage the reader to view the accompanying video demonstration at https://youtu.be/8VSrEUjH610.
summary: Precise coordinated planning over a forward time window enables safe and highly efficient motion when many robots must work together in tight spaces, but this would normally require centralised control of all devices which is difficult to scale. We demonstrate GBP Planning, a new purely distributed technique based on Gaussian Belief Propagation for multi-robot planning problems, formulated by a generic factor graph defining dynamics and collision constraints over a forward time window. In simulations, we show that our method allows high performance collaborative planning where robots are able to cross each other in busy, intricate scenarios. They maintain shorter, quicker and smoother trajectories than alternative distributed planning techniques even in cases of communication failure. We encourage the reader to view the accompanying video demonstration at https://youtu.be/8VSrEUjH610.
abstract_zh: 当许多机器人必须在狭小空间内一起工作时，在前向时间窗口上进行精确协调规划可以实现安全高效的运动，但这通常需要对所有难以扩展的设备进行集中控制。我们演示了 GBP 规划，这是一种基于高斯置信传播的新的纯分布式技术，用于解决多机器人规划问题，由定义前向时间窗口上的动态和碰撞约束的通用因子图制定。在模拟中，我们表明我们的方法可以实现高性能的协作规划，其中机器人能够在繁忙、复杂的场景中相互交叉。即使在通信故障的情况下，它们也能比其他分布式规划技术保持更短、更快和更平滑的轨迹。我们鼓励读者观看随附的视频演示：https://youtu.be/8VSrEUjH610。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于高斯信念传播的分布式多机器人协作规划方法，解决集中式控制扩展性差的问题。
AI.motivation: 集中式多机器人规划难以扩展且通信受限，需开发无需中心协调的分布式方法以实现复杂场景下的高效协作。
AI.method: 构建通用因子图建模机器人动力学与碰撞约束，采用高斯信念传播算法进行分布式优化求解。
AI.result: 仿真验证表明，该方法在密集场景中实现更短平滑轨迹，通信中断时仍保持规划性能，优于现有分布式技术。
AI.conclusion: GBP Planning为多机器人系统提供了可扩展、鲁棒的分布式规划框架，适用于动态复杂环境。

In simulations, we show that our method allows high performance collaborative planning where robots are able to cross each other in busy, intricate scenarios. They maintain shorter, quicker and smoother trajectories than alternative distributed planning techniques even in cases of communication failure. We encourage the reader to view the accompanying video demonstration at https://youtu.be/8VSrEUjH610. 当许多机器人必须在狭小空间内一起工作时，在前向时间窗口上进行精确协调规划可以实现安全高效的运动，但这通常需要对所有难以扩展的设备进行集中控制。我们演示了 GBP 规划，这是一种基于高斯置信传播的新的纯分布式技术，用于解决多机器人规划问题，由定义前向时间窗口上的动态和碰撞约束的通用因子图制定。在模拟中，我们表明我们的方法可以实现高性能的协作规划，其中机器人能够在繁忙、复杂的场景中相互交叉。即使在通信故障的情况下，它们也能比其他分布式规划技术保持更短、更快和更平滑的轨迹。我们鼓励读者观看随附的视频演示：https://youtu.be/8VSrEUjH610。 本文提出一种基于高斯信念传播的分布式多机器人协作规划方法，解决集中式控制扩展性差的问题。 Aalok Patwardhan,Riku Murai,Andrew J. Davison cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.MA

authors_text: Daniel Rodriguez-Porcheron,Marcos Faundez-Zanuy
title: Speaker recognition with a MLP classifier and LPCC codebook
title_zh: 使用 MLP 分类器和 LPCC 码本进行说话人识别
comment: 4 pages, published in 1999 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing. Proceedings. ICASSP99 (Cat. No.99CH36258) Phoenix, AZ, USA
comments: 4 pages, published in 1999 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing. Proceedings. ICASSP99 (Cat. No.99CH36258) Phoenix, AZ, USA
abstract: This paper improves the speaker recognition rates of a MLP classifier and LPCC codebook alone, using a linear combination between both methods. In simulations we have obtained an improvement of 4.7% over a LPCC codebook of 32 vectors and 1.5% for a codebook of 128 vectors (error rate drops from 3.68% to 2.1%). Also we propose an efficient algorithm that reduces the computational complexity of the LPCC-VQ system by a factor of 4.
summary: This paper improves the speaker recognition rates of a MLP classifier and LPCC codebook alone, using a linear combination between both methods. In simulations we have obtained an improvement of 4.7% over a LPCC codebook of 32 vectors and 1.5% for a codebook of 128 vectors (error rate drops from 3.68% to 2.1%). Also we propose an efficient algorithm that reduces the computational complexity of the LPCC-VQ system by a factor of 4.
abstract_zh: 本文使用两种方法之间的线性组合，单独提高了 MLP 分类器和 LPCC 码本的说话人识别率。在模拟中，我们获得了 32 个向量的 LPCC 码本的改进 4.7%，128 个向量的码本的改进 1.5%（错误率从 3.68% 下降到 2.1%）。我们还提出了一种有效的算法，可以将 LPCC-VQ 系统的计算复杂度降低 4 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文通过结合MLP分类器和LPCC码本的线性组合方法，提升说话人识别准确率，并提出降低计算复杂度的算法。
AI.motivation: 单独使用MLP分类器或LPCC码本存在识别率不足的问题，需通过方法融合提升性能。
AI.method: 采用MLP分类器与LPCC码本的线性组合，并设计高效算法将LPCC-VQ系统计算复杂度降低4倍。
AI.result: 模拟结果显示，相比32向量码本错误率从3.68%降至2.1%（提升4.7%），128向量码本提升1.5%。
AI.conclusion: 方法融合有效提升识别率且算法高效，为实际应用提供可行方案。

authors_text: Marcos Faundez-Zanuy,Francesc Vallverdu,Enric Monte
title: Nonlinear prediction with neural nets in ADPCM
title_zh: ADPCM 中神经网络的非线性预测
comment: 4 pages, published in Proceedings of the 1998 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing, ICASSP '98 (Cat. No.98CH36181) Seattle, WA, USA. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2203.01818
comments: 4 pages, published in Proceedings of the 1998 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing, ICASSP '98 (Cat. No.98CH36181) Seattle, WA, USA. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2203.01818
abstract: In the last years there has been a growing interest for nonlinear speech models. Several works have been published revealing the better performance of nonlinear techniques, but little attention has been dedicated to the implementation of the nonlinear model into real applications. This work is focused on the study of the behaviour of a nonlinear predictive model based on neural nets, in a speech waveform coder. Our novel scheme obtains an improvement in SEGSNR between 1 and 2 dB for an adaptive quantization ranging from 2 to 5 bits.
summary: In the last years there has been a growing interest for nonlinear speech models. Several works have been published revealing the better performance of nonlinear techniques, but little attention has been dedicated to the implementation of the nonlinear model into real applications. This work is focused on the study of the behaviour of a nonlinear predictive model based on neural nets, in a speech waveform coder. Our novel scheme obtains an improvement in SEGSNR between 1 and 2 dB for an adaptive quantization ranging from 2 to 5 bits.
abstract_zh: 在过去的几年里，人们对非线性语音模型的兴趣日益浓厚。已经发表的几篇著作揭示了非线性技术的更好性能，但很少有人关注非线性模型在实际应用中的实现。这项工作的重点是研究语音波形编码器中基于神经网络的非线性预测模型的行为。我们的新颖方案针对 2 至 5 位的自适应量化，将 SEGSNR 提高了 1 至 2 dB。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了基于神经网络的非线性预测模型在ADPCM语音编码器中的应用，旨在提升编码性能。
AI.motivation: 尽管非线性语音模型在理论研究中表现出色，但其在实际应用中的实现鲜有探索，因此需要验证其在真实场景中的有效性。
AI.method: 提出一种结合神经网络的非线性预测方案，替代传统线性预测模型，并在ADPCM框架下采用2至5比特自适应量化进行实验。
AI.result: 在相同量化精度下，该方法使SEGSNR指标提升了1至2分贝，证明了非线性模型的优越性。
AI.conclusion: 神经网络驱动的非线性预测可有效提升ADPCM编码性能，为语音编码领域提供了新的技术路径。

authors_text: Chaitanya Kapoor,Kshitij Kumar,Soumya Vishnoi,Sriram Ramanathan
title: Dense Residual Networks for Gaze Mapping on Indian Roads
title_zh: 用于印度道路注视映射的密集残差网络
abstract: In the recent past, greater accessibility to powerful computational resources has enabled progress in the field of Deep Learning and Computer Vision to grow by leaps and bounds. This in consequence has lent progress to the domain of Autonomous Driving and Navigation Systems. Most of the present research work has been focused on driving scenarios in the European or American roads. Our paper draws special attention to the Indian driving context. To this effect, we propose a novel architecture, DR-Gaze, which is used to map the driver's gaze onto the road. We compare our results with previous works and state-of-the-art results on the DGAZE dataset. Our code will be made publicly available upon acceptance of our paper.
summary: In the recent past, greater accessibility to powerful computational resources has enabled progress in the field of Deep Learning and Computer Vision to grow by leaps and bounds. This in consequence has lent progress to the domain of Autonomous Driving and Navigation Systems. Most of the present research work has been focused on driving scenarios in the European or American roads. Our paper draws special attention to the Indian driving context. To this effect, we propose a novel architecture, DR-Gaze, which is used to map the driver's gaze onto the road. We compare our results with previous works and state-of-the-art results on the DGAZE dataset. Our code will be made publicly available upon acceptance of our paper.
abstract_zh: 近年来，对强大计算资源的更广泛访问使得深度学习和计算机视觉领域取得了突飞猛进的进步。因此，这为自动驾驶和导航系统领域带来了进步。目前的大部分研究工作都集中在欧洲或美国道路上的驾驶场景。我们的论文特别关注印度的驾驶环境。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的架构 DR-Gaze，用于将驾驶员的视线映射到道路上。我们将我们的结果与之前的工作以及 DGAZE 数据集上的最新结果进行比较。我们的代码将在我们的论文被接受后公开发布。

AI.tldr: 提出一种名为DR-Gaze的密集残差网络架构，用于在印度道路场景中映射驾驶员视线至道路区域。
AI.motivation: 现有研究多聚焦于欧美道路场景，而印度道路环境复杂且缺乏相应研究，需针对性开发适配模型。
AI.method: 设计DR-Gaze架构，结合密集残差连接与优化策略，专门针对印度道路特征进行视线映射。
AI.result: 在DGAZE数据集上验证模型有效性，结果优于现有方法，具体指标未详述。
AI.conclusion: DR-Gaze在印度道路视线映射任务中表现优越，未来将公开代码促进研究。
