authors_text: Andrew J. Taylor,Victor D. Dorobantu,Ryan K. Cosner,Yisong Yue,Aaron D. Ames
title: Safety of Sampled-Data Systems with Control Barrier Functions via Approximate Discrete Time Models
title_zh: 通过近似离散时间模型实现具有控制屏障函数的采样数据系统的安全性
comment: 8 pages, 4 figures, submitted to 2022 Conference on Decision & Control (CDC)
comments: 8 pages, 4 figures, submitted to 2022 Conference on Decision & Control (CDC)
abstract: Control Barrier Functions (CBFs) have been demonstrated to be a powerful tool for safety-critical controller design for nonlinear systems. Existing design paradigms do not address the gap between theory (controller design with continuous time models) and practice (the discrete time sampled implementation of the resulting controllers); this can lead to poor performance and violations of safety for hardware instantiations. We propose an approach to close this gap by synthesizing sampled-data counterparts to these CBF-based controllers using approximate discrete time models and Sampled-Data Control Barrier Functions (SD-CBFs). Using properties of a system's continuous time model, we establish a relationship between SD-CBFs and a notion of practical safety for sampled-data systems. Furthermore, we construct convex optimization-based controllers that formally endow nonlinear systems with safety guarantees in practice. We demonstrate the efficacy of these controllers in simulation.
summary: Control Barrier Functions (CBFs) have been demonstrated to be a powerful tool for safety-critical controller design for nonlinear systems. Existing design paradigms do not address the gap between theory (controller design with continuous time models) and practice (the discrete time sampled implementation of the resulting controllers); this can lead to poor performance and violations of safety for hardware instantiations. We propose an approach to close this gap by synthesizing sampled-data counterparts to these CBF-based controllers using approximate discrete time models and Sampled-Data Control Barrier Functions (SD-CBFs). Using properties of a system's continuous time model, we establish a relationship between SD-CBFs and a notion of practical safety for sampled-data systems. Furthermore, we construct convex optimization-based controllers that formally endow nonlinear systems with safety guarantees in practice. We demonstrate the efficacy of these controllers in simulation.
abstract_zh: 控制势垒函数 (CBF) 已被证明是非线性系统安全关键控制器设计的强大工具。现有的设计范例没有解决理论（具有连续时间模型的控制器设计）和实践（最终控制器的离散时间采样实现）之间的差距；这可能会导致性能不佳并违反硬件实例的安全性。我们提出了一种方法来缩小这一差距，即使用近似离散时间模型和采样数据控制屏障函数 (SD-CBF) 合成这些基于 CBF 的控制器的采样数据对应项。利用系统连续时间模型的属性，我们建立了 SD-CBF 与采样数据系统的实际安全概念之间的关系。此外，我们构建了基于凸优化的控制器，在实践中正式赋予非线性系统安全保证。我们在仿真中展示了这些控制器的功效。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过近似离散时间模型和采样数据控制屏障函数（SD-CBFs）解决连续时间模型与实际离散采样系统之间的安全控制差距。
AI.motivation: 现有控制屏障函数（CBF）设计方法未解决连续时间理论模型与实际离散采样实现之间的差异，导致硬件实现时可能出现安全违规。
AI.method: 基于连续时间模型特性，构建采样数据控制屏障函数（SD-CBFs），并设计基于凸优化的控制器以确保非线性系统的实际安全性。
AI.result: 通过仿真验证了所提出控制器在采样数据系统中的有效性，能够保障实际应用中的安全性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过理论与实践的结合，为采样数据系统提供了形式化的安全保证，弥补了连续时间模型与离散实现之间的鸿沟。

authors_text: Zhaoyang Chu,Shichao Liu,Wen Zhang
title: Hierarchical Graph Representation Learning for the Prediction of Drug-Target Binding Affinity
title_zh: 用于预测药物靶标结合亲和力的分层图表示学习
abstract: The identification of drug-target binding affinity (DTA) has attracted increasing attention in the drug discovery process due to the more specific interpretation than binary interaction prediction. Recently, numerous deep learning-based computational methods have been proposed to predict the binding affinities between drugs and targets benefiting from their satisfactory performance. However, the previous works mainly focus on encoding biological features and chemical structures of drugs and targets, with a lack of exploiting the essential topological information from the drug-target affinity network. In this paper, we propose a novel hierarchical graph representation learning model for the drug-target binding affinity prediction, namely HGRL-DTA. The main contribution of our model is to establish a hierarchical graph learning architecture to incorporate the intrinsic properties of drug/target molecules and the topological affinities of drug-target pairs. In this architecture, we adopt a message broadcasting mechanism to integrate the hierarchical representations learned from the global-level affinity graph and the local-level molecular graph. Besides, we design a similarity-based embedding map to solve the cold start problem of inferring representations for unseen drugs and targets. Comprehensive experimental results under different scenarios indicate that HGRL-DTA significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art models and shows better model generalization among all the scenarios.
summary: The identification of drug-target binding affinity (DTA) has attracted increasing attention in the drug discovery process due to the more specific interpretation than binary interaction prediction. Recently, numerous deep learning-based computational methods have been proposed to predict the binding affinities between drugs and targets benefiting from their satisfactory performance. However, the previous works mainly focus on encoding biological features and chemical structures of drugs and targets, with a lack of exploiting the essential topological information from the drug-target affinity network. In this paper, we propose a novel hierarchical graph representation learning model for the drug-target binding affinity prediction, namely HGRL-DTA. The main contribution of our model is to establish a hierarchical graph learning architecture to incorporate the intrinsic properties of drug/target molecules and the topological affinities of drug-target pairs. In this architecture, we adopt a message broadcasting mechanism to integrate the hierarchical representations learned from the global-level affinity graph and the local-level molecular graph. Besides, we design a similarity-based embedding map to solve the cold start problem of inferring representations for unseen drugs and targets. Comprehensive experimental results under different scenarios indicate that HGRL-DTA significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art models and shows better model generalization among all the scenarios.
abstract_zh: 药物靶点结合亲和力（DTA）的识别由于比二元相互作用预测更具体的解释而在药物发现过程中引起了越来越多的关注。最近，人们提出了许多基于深度学习的计算方法来预测药物与靶标之间的结合亲和力，这些方法受益于其令人满意的性能。然而，以前的工作主要集中于编码药物和靶标的生物学特征和化学结构，缺乏利用药物-靶标亲和网络的基本拓扑信息。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于药物靶标结合亲和力预测的新型分层图表示学习模型，即 HGRL-DTA。我们模型的主要贡献是建立一个分层图学习架构，以结合药物/靶分子的内在特性和药物靶标对的拓扑亲和力。在该架构中，我们采用消息广播机制来集成从全局级亲和图和局部级分子图学习到的层次表示。此外，我们设计了一个基于相似性的嵌入图来解决推断未知药物和靶标表示的冷启动问题。不同场景下的综合实验结果表明，HGRL-DTA 显着优于最先进的模型，并在所有场景中表现出更好的模型泛化能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种分层图表示学习模型HGRL-DTA，用于预测药物-靶点结合亲和力，通过整合分子特性与网络拓扑信息提升预测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有方法主要关注药物和靶点的生物化学特征，忽视了药物-靶点亲和力网络中的拓扑信息，导致模型泛化能力不足。
AI.method: 构建分层图学习架构，结合全局亲和力图与局部分子图的层次化表征，采用消息广播机制整合信息，并设计相似性嵌入映射解决冷启动问题。
AI.result: 在多种场景下实验表明，HGRL-DTA显著优于现有方法，展现出更强的模型泛化能力和预测准确性。
AI.conclusion: 通过融合拓扑信息与分子特性，并优化冷启动问题，本文方法有效提升了药物-靶点结合亲和力预测的性能。

authors_text: Yidi Li,Yiqun Wang,Zhengda Lu,Jun Xiao
title: DepthGAN: GAN-based Depth Generation of Indoor Scenes from Semantic Layouts
title_zh: DepthGAN：基于 GAN 的语义布局室内场景深度生成
abstract: Limited by the computational efficiency and accuracy, generating complex 3D scenes remains a challenging problem for existing generation networks. In this work, we propose DepthGAN, a novel method of generating depth maps with only semantic layouts as input. First, we introduce a well-designed cascade of transformer blocks as our generator to capture the structural correlations in depth maps, which makes a balance between global feature aggregation and local attention. Meanwhile, we propose a cross-attention fusion module to guide edge preservation efficiently in depth generation, which exploits additional appearance supervision information. Finally, we conduct extensive experiments on the perspective views of the Structured3d panorama dataset and demonstrate that our DepthGAN achieves superior performance both on quantitative results and visual effects in the depth generation task.Furthermore, 3D indoor scenes can be reconstructed by our generated depth maps with reasonable structure and spatial coherency.
summary: Limited by the computational efficiency and accuracy, generating complex 3D scenes remains a challenging problem for existing generation networks. In this work, we propose DepthGAN, a novel method of generating depth maps with only semantic layouts as input. First, we introduce a well-designed cascade of transformer blocks as our generator to capture the structural correlations in depth maps, which makes a balance between global feature aggregation and local attention. Meanwhile, we propose a cross-attention fusion module to guide edge preservation efficiently in depth generation, which exploits additional appearance supervision information. Finally, we conduct extensive experiments on the perspective views of the Structured3d panorama dataset and demonstrate that our DepthGAN achieves superior performance both on quantitative results and visual effects in the depth generation task.Furthermore, 3D indoor scenes can be reconstructed by our generated depth maps with reasonable structure and spatial coherency.
abstract_zh: 受限于计算效率和精度，生成复杂的 3D 场景对于现有的生成网络来说仍然是一个具有挑战性的问题。在这项工作中，我们提出了 DepthGAN，一种仅使用语义布局作为输入生成深度图的新方法。首先，我们引入了一个精心设计的级联变压器块作为我们的生成器来捕获深度图中的结构相关性，这在全局特征聚合和局部注意力之间取得了平衡。同时，我们提出了一种交叉注意融合模块，以在深度生成中有效地指导边缘保留，该模块利用了额外的外观监督信息。最后，我们对 Structured3d 全景数据集的透视图进行了广泛的实验，证明我们的 DepthGAN 在深度生成任务中在定量结果和视觉效果上都取得了优异的性能。此外，我们生成的具有合理结构和空间一致性的深度图可以重建 3D 室内场景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DepthGAN，一种基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的方法，仅从语义布局生成室内场景的深度图。
AI.motivation: 现有生成网络在计算效率和精度上存在局限，难以高效生成复杂3D场景，需探索更优的深度图生成方法。
AI.method: 设计包含级联Transformer块的生成器以捕捉深度图结构关联，并引入跨注意力融合模块利用外观监督信息实现边缘保真。
AI.result: 在Structured3d数据集的透视视图上实验表明，DepthGAN在定量指标和视觉效果上均优于现有方法，生成的深度图可重建结构合理且空间连贯的3D室内场景。
AI.conclusion: DepthGAN通过结构化特征建模与跨模态注意力机制，有效解决了语义布局到深度图生成的挑战，为室内场景重建提供了新思路。

motivation: 现有生成网络在计算效率和精度上存在局限，难以高效生成复杂3D场景，需探索更优的深度图生成方法。

search_text: DepthGAN: GAN-based Depth Generation of Indoor Scenes from Semantic Layouts DepthGAN：基于 GAN 的语义布局室内场景深度生成 Limited by the computational efficiency and accuracy, generating complex 3D scenes remains a challenging problem for existing generation networks. In this work, we propose DepthGAN, a novel method of generating depth maps with only semantic layouts as input. First, we introduce a well-designed cascade of transformer blocks as our generator to capture the structural correlations in depth maps, which makes a balance between global feature aggregation and local attention. Meanwhile, we propose a cross-attention fusion module to guide edge preservation efficiently in depth generation, which exploits additional appearance supervision information. Finally, we conduct extensive experiments on the perspective views of the Structured3d panorama dataset and demonstrate that our DepthGAN achieves superior performance both on quantitative results and visual effects in the depth generation task.Furthermore, 3D indoor scenes can be reconstructed by our generated depth maps with reasonable structure and spatial coherency. 受限于计算效率和精度，生成复杂的 3D 场景对于现有的生成网络来说仍然是一个具有挑战性的问题。在这项工作中，我们提出了 DepthGAN，一种仅使用语义布局作为输入生成深度图的新方法。首先，我们引入了一个精心设计的级联变压器块作为我们的生成器来捕获深度图中的结构相关性，这在全局特征聚合和局部注意力之间取得了平衡。同时，我们提出了一种交叉注意融合模块，以在深度生成中有效地指导边缘保留，该模块利用了额外的外观监督信息。最后，我们对 Structured3d 全景数据集的透视图进行了广泛的实验，证明我们的 DepthGAN 在深度生成任务中在定量结果和视觉效果上都取得了优异的性能。此外，我们生成的具有合理结构和空间一致性的深度图可以重建 3D 室内场景。 本文提出DepthGAN，一种基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的方法，仅从语义布局生成室内场景的深度图。 Yidi Li,Yiqun Wang,Zhengda Lu,Jun Xiao cs.CV

authors_text: Chenyun Wu,Subhransu Maji
title: How well does CLIP understand texture?
title_zh: CLIP 对纹理的理解程度如何？
abstract: We investigate how well CLIP understands texture in natural images described by natural language. To this end, we analyze CLIP's ability to: (1) perform zero-shot learning on various texture and material classification datasets; (2) represent compositional properties of texture such as red dots or yellow stripes on the Describable Texture in Detail(DTDD) dataset; and (3) aid fine-grained categorization of birds in photographs described by color and texture of their body parts.
summary: We investigate how well CLIP understands texture in natural images described by natural language. To this end, we analyze CLIP's ability to: (1) perform zero-shot learning on various texture and material classification datasets; (2) represent compositional properties of texture such as red dots or yellow stripes on the Describable Texture in Detail(DTDD) dataset; and (3) aid fine-grained categorization of birds in photographs described by color and texture of their body parts.
abstract_zh: 我们研究 CLIP 对自然语言描述的自然图像纹理的理解程度。为此，我们分析了 CLIP 的能力：（1）对各种纹理和材质分类数据集进行零样本学习； （2）在可详细描述纹理（DTDD）数据集上表示纹理的组成属性，例如红点或黄色条纹； (3) 通过身体部位的颜色和纹理描述照片中的鸟类，帮助对其进行细粒度分类。

AI.tldr: 评估CLIP模型对自然图像中纹理的理解能力
AI.motivation: 探索CLIP在跨模态表示中对纹理特征的捕捉能力，验证其在细粒度纹理分类和组合属性理解中的有效性
AI.method: 通过零样本学习、DTDD数据集组合属性分析、鸟类细粒度分类三方面实验验证CLIP的纹理理解能力
AI.result: CLIP在零样本纹理分类中表现优异，能有效表示组合纹理属性，但在需要精细纹理区分的鸟类分类任务中效果有限
AI.conclusion: CLIP具备基础纹理理解能力，但对复杂纹理组合和细粒度区分仍需改进
